You are auditing source code for an INDEPENDENT external code review (client: Telus). Tools: Bash, Read.

STEP 0 — SKIP IF DONE. Run: test -s /tmp/claude-1000/-home-polaris-polaris-project/21e87760-8436-4090-870d-99ef2121882e/scratchpad/name_findings/shard_2.tsv && echo DONE || echo TODO
If it prints DONE, this shard is already complete — reply exactly "shard 2: cached" and STOP. Do nothing else.

STEP 1 — get your file list: /opt/conda/bin/python -c "import json; print(chr(10).join(json.load(open('/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/.name_shards.json'))[2]))"
It prints lines "relpath:START-END" relative to /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent.

STEP 2 — GENUINELY READ every assigned file/range line by line with the Read tool (offset/limit from START-END; use limit up to 2000 per call to minimize calls). Do NOT grep-and-skim — read the code.

STEP 3 — flag EVERY name (file/module, class, func/method, var, const, enum value) that looks UNPROFESSIONAL in an external review:
  - marketing/quality adjectives: honest, real, true, smart, magic, ultimate, enhanced, improved, robust, aggressive, killer, awesome, blazing, super, mega, best
  - temporal/version markers: v2/v3/v4, r2/r3, _new, _old, legacy, deprecated, next/nextgen, final(meaning 'this version'), fixed, temp, wip, draft, backup, orig, working, experimental, proto
  - jokes/informal/vague/slang: junk, garbage, hack, spy, stub(as a real name), yolo, stuff, misc, foo/bar, doit, thing, blah, landmine, nuke, magic-number-as-word, terse cryptic abbreviations
Judge at a STRICT bar: **when in doubt, mark RENAME.** Only KEEP-BUT-NOTED for unambiguously standard, descriptive, professional vocabulary or a REAL domain term genuinely implemented (finalize, proper_noun, evict_older_than, SmartArt only if it truly makes SmartArt).

STEP 4 — APPEND TAB-separated rows to /tmp/claude-1000/-home-polaris-polaris-project/21e87760-8436-4090-870d-99ef2121882e/scratchpad/name_findings/shard_2.tsv (one per flagged name), using printf with real tabs:
  VERDICT<TAB>NAME<TAB>relpath:LINE<TAB>KIND<TAB>WHY<TAB>SUGGESTED_NAME
  VERDICT=RENAME or KEEP-NOTED; KIND=file/class/func/var/const/enum. If a file is fully clean, write: NONE<TAB>-<TAB>relpath<TAB>-<TAB>clean<TAB>-

Reply ONLY: "shard 2: <R> renames, <K> kept across <F> files".

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""STEP 15: compose the REAL scoreable multi-section report from the LIVE agentic run.
3	
4	The mission's metric-(a) full-corpus gate (cp4_used=agentic on the 329-basket corpus) already
5	PASSED (docs/agentic_sweep_live_summary_s3gear329.json). What was missing was a *composed*
6	report we can score. This driver closes that gap: it runs the FULL generator
7	(``generate_multi_section_report``) with the agentic outliner ON (PG_OUTLINE_AGENT=1 + the
8	§9.1.8 model lock) over data/cp4_corpus_s3gear_329.json and writes report.md.
9	
10	Model it on scripts/run_honest_on_prerebuild_corpus.py (which already produced report.md +
11	multi_section_outline.json), minus the retrieval/scope machinery (the corpus is pre-built).
12	
13	Faithfulness gate (HARD): after composition, assert ZERO unverified numbers reach any
14	[CITE:ev_xxx] token in the composed report. The strict_verify lane already enforces this
15	per-section; this driver re-audits the final assembled text as an independent tripwire.
16	
17	Run (key MUST be in env):
18	    set -a && . ./.env && set +a
19	    PG_OUTLINE_AGENT=1 python scripts/compose_agentic_report_s3gear329.py \
20	        --corpus data/cp4_corpus_s3gear_329.json
21	"""
22	from __future__ import annotations
23	
24	import argparse
25	import asyncio
26	import json
27	import logging
28	import os
29	import re
30	import sys
31	import time
32	from pathlib import Path
33	from types import SimpleNamespace
34	
35	ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
36	sys.path.insert(0, str(ROOT))
37	
38	DRB_QUERY = ROOT / "third_party" / "deep_research_bench" / "data" / "prompt_data" / "query.jsonl"
39	
40	# STEP 2 (wheel: topic-driven structure) — the section headings are now produced TOPIC-DRIVEN by
41	# the generator itself (facet outline + general research-report skeleton: PG_FACET_OUTLINE=1 +
42	# PG_FACET_OUTLINE_SKELETON=1). The prior STEP-16 approach hardcoded a clinical-archetype ->
43	# AI/labor relabel MAP here — an overfit band-aid tuned to one benchmark task. That map is GONE:
44	# the outliner emits real topical titles (Introduction / thematic bodies / Cross-Study Synthesis /
45	# Conclusions and Research Gaps) for ANY domain, so assembly renders the section titles verbatim.
46	
47	
48	def _derive_title(rq: str) -> str:
49	    """Derive a neutral report title from the research question — GENERAL, not tuned to any task.
50	
51	    Takes the first sentence/clause of the RQ, strips a leading imperative ("Please write a ...",
52	    "Research ...", "I am researching ..."), and Title-cases nothing (keeps the RQ's own wording).
53	    Falls back to a generic label. No topic is hardcoded."""
54	    import re as _re
55	    s = (rq or "").strip().replace("\n", " ")
56	    s = _re.sub(r"\s+", " ", s)
57	    # First sentence only.
58	    s = _re.split(r"(?<=[.?!])\s", s, maxsplit=1)[0]
59	    # Strip common leading imperatives so the title reads as a subject, not a command.
60	    s = _re.sub(r"^(please\s+)?(help me\s+)?(write|prepare|produce|conduct|research(ing)?|"
61	                r"provide|create|complete|collect( and)?( organi[sz]e)?|i am researching|"
62	                r"i would like|i need)\b[:,]?\s*", "", s, flags=_re.IGNORECASE)
63	    s = s.strip().rstrip(".").strip()
64	    if not s:
65	        return "Research Report"
66	    # Capitalize the first letter only (preserve proper-noun casing in the rest).
67	    return s[0].upper() + s[1:]
68	
69	
70	def _load_drb_prompt(task_id: str) -> str:
71	    """Load a DeepResearch-Bench task's EXACT prompt verbatim (target/ref/criteria all key on it)."""
72	    for line in DRB_QUERY.read_text().splitlines():
73	        o = json.loads(line)
74	        if str(o.get("id")) == str(task_id):
75	            return o["prompt"]
76	    raise SystemExit(f"BLOCKED: DRB task id {task_id} not in {DRB_QUERY}")
77	
78	logging.basicConfig(
79	    level=os.environ.get("PG_LOG_LEVEL", "INFO"),
80	    format="%(asctime)s %(name)s %(levelname)s %(message)s",
81	    datefmt="%H:%M:%S",
82	    stream=sys.stdout,
83	)
84	for noisy in ("httpx", "httpcore"):
85	    logging.getLogger(noisy).setLevel(logging.WARNING)
86	log = logging.getLogger("compose")
87	
88	
89	def _tier_fractions(evidence: list[dict]) -> dict[str, float]:
90	    from collections import Counter
91	    c = Counter((e.get("tier") or "T?").upper() for e in evidence)
92	    n = sum(c.values()) or 1
93	    return {k: v / n for k, v in sorted(c.items())}
94	
95	
96	# A numeric token that would be a faithfulness breach if it sat inside a [CITE:] sentence
97	# without having passed strict_verify. We audit the FINAL assembled report: any [CITE:ev_xxx]
98	# in the verified text is, by construction, already span-grounded — but we re-scan to prove it.
99	_CITE_RE = re.compile(r"\[CITE:(ev_[0-9a-fA-F]+|[a-z0-9_]+)\]")
100	
101	
102	def _audit_citations(report_text: str, biblio: list[dict]) -> dict:
103	    """Independent faithfulness tripwire on the FINAL assembled report.
104	
105	    strict_verify resolves every kept sentence's provenance token into a global [N] bibliography
106	    marker and DROPS any sentence whose number failed the span match. So in a faithful final
107	    report: (1) ZERO raw [CITE:ev_xxx] tokens survive (any survivor is an unverified-number leak
108	    — the exact breach the mission forbids), and (2) every [N] marker in the prose resolves to a
109	    real bibliography entry. We assert both."""
110	    leaked_cites = _CITE_RE.findall(report_text)
111	    body = report_text.split("\n\n## References\n", 1)[0]  # markers in prose only
112	    n_markers = set(int(m) for m in re.findall(r"\[(\d+)\]", body))
113	    biblio_nums = {int(b.get("num")) for b in biblio if str(b.get("num", "")).isdigit()}
114	    unresolved = sorted(n for n in n_markers if n not in biblio_nums)
115	    return {
116	        "leaked_cite_ev_tokens": len(leaked_cites),
117	        "leaked_cite_samples": sorted(set(leaked_cites))[:10],
118	        "distinct_bib_markers_in_prose": len(n_markers),
119	        "bibliography_entries": len(biblio_nums),
120	        "unresolved_markers": unresolved,
121	    }
122	
123	
124	def _order_sections_by_required(verified: list, required_titles: list[str]) -> list:
125	    """Reorder VERIFIED sections so those matching the required titles come first,
126	    in the required order; all others keep their original relative order after.
127	
128	    Matching is case-insensitive containment (a required title matched against a
129	    produced heading). ORDER-ONLY: every input section is present in the output
130	    exactly once — nothing is dropped or fabricated. A required title with no
131	    matching verified section is simply skipped (disclosure is the audit's job)."""
132	    want = [(t or "").strip().lower() for t in required_titles if (t or "").strip()]
133	    used: set[int] = set()
134	    ordered: list = []
135	    for w in want:
136	        for i, sr in enumerate(verified):
137	            if i in used:
138	                continue
139	            tl = (sr.title or "").strip().lower()
140	            if tl == w or w in tl or tl in w:
141	                ordered.append(sr)
142	                used.add(i)
143	                break
144	    for i, sr in enumerate(verified):
145	        if i not in used:
146	            ordered.append(sr)
147	    return ordered
148	
149	
150	def _dedup_biblio_by_work(biblio: list[dict]) -> list[dict]:
151	    """Collapse bibliography rows that resolve to the same underlying WORK.
152	
153	    Key is the normalized URL when present, else the normalized statement text.
154	    The FIRST row for each work is kept (preserving its assigned number); later
155	    duplicates are removed. This drops only DUPLICATE reference lines — never a
156	    distinct source — and does not touch in-prose [N] markers."""
157	    seen: set[str] = set()
158	    out: list[dict] = []
159	    for b in biblio:
160	        url = str(b.get("url", "") or "").strip().lower().rstrip("/")
161	        key = url or str(b.get("statement", "") or "").strip().lower()[:160]
162	        if key and key in seen:
163	            continue
164	        if key:
165	            seen.add(key)
166	        out.append(b)
167	    return out
168	
169	
170	async def main() -> int:
171	    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=__doc__)
172	    ap.add_argument("--corpus", required=True)
173	    ap.add_argument("--out-dir", default=None)
174	    ap.add_argument("--max-parallel", type=int, default=3)
175	    ap.add_argument("--rq-drb-task", default="72",
176	                    help="override the corpus RQ with this DRB task's verbatim prompt so the "
177	                         "composed report answers the SAME task it is scored against; empty string "
178	                         "keeps the corpus RQ")
179	    ap.add_argument("--title", default=None,
180	                    help="report title for the judged report.md; default DERIVES it from the RQ "
181	                         "(general — no title is hardcoded to any task)")
182	    # S4 render projection (default-OFF): when a pinned planning_gate_artifact.json is
183	    # supplied, thread its deliverable/scope/compose projections into composition and
184	    # run the disclosure-only contract-compliance audit alongside the (untouched)
185	    # citation audit. Omitted (the default) => byte-identical to today's champion.
186	    ap.add_argument("--gate-artifact", default=None,
187	                    help="OPTIONAL path to a pinned planning_gate_artifact.json. When given, its "
188	                         "contract projections steer contract-aware assembly (required sections/"
189	                         "order, references dedup) and the compose voice, and audit_contract runs. "
190	                         "Absent => byte-identical champion behavior (no gate).")
191	    args = ap.parse_args()
192	
193	    if not os.getenv("OPENROUTER_API_KEY"):
194	        log.error("BLOCKED: OPENROUTER_API_KEY not in env — source .env first "
195	                  "(set -a && . ./.env && set +a)")
196	        return 2
197	    # The mission model-lock: agentic outliner ON.
198	    os.environ.setdefault("PG_OUTLINE_AGENT", "1")
199	    # P0 CONFIRMED-SAFE COMPOSE CONFIG (2026-07-12) — PIN the non-deadlocking config in the launch
200	    # path. The clean 24.2min/1449.7s run used exactly this: off-loop ON (shipped, verdict-safe),
201	    # PG_COMPOSE_BASKET_WORKERS=1 (serial byte-identical MAP+REDUCE — NEVER >1 without a full-328
202	    # verdict-identity A/B), PG_SIDE_JUDGE_MAX_CONCURRENCY in the 4-8 band (NEVER >=48), and
203	    # PG_PARALLEL_SECTIONS=3. These are setdefault (an explicit operator override still wins) but they
204	    # keep this driver on the certified-safe path; the startup guard (compose_config_guard) refuses the
205	    # deadlocking regime regardless. Faithfulness-neutral: pure concurrency knobs.
206	    os.environ.setdefault("PG_COMPOSE_BASKET_WORKERS", "1")
207	    os.environ.setdefault("PG_SIDE_JUDGE_MAX_CONCURRENCY", "8")
208	    os.environ.setdefault("PG_PARALLEL_SECTIONS", "3")
209	    # P1-SPEED (2026-07-12) — collapse the ISOLATED pre-compose credibility member-verify pass.
210	    # ROOT-CAUSE of the 43min (2589.7s) >> 24min (1449.7s) gap, MEASURED from the phase timeline in
211	    # logs/step3_full328_render.log: threading the PSL gov_suffixes (below) to lift route_all basket
212	    # utilization ALSO activates the ADVISORY credibility corroboration pass. On this 997-member corpus
213	    # that pass ran SERIALLY (PG_CREDIBILITY_PASS_MAX_INFLIGHT default=1) and BANKED at its
214	    # wall*0.85 soft deadline = 1020s, verifying only 207/997 members — a full +1020s phase the 1449.7s
215	    # baseline NEVER ran (it did not thread gov_suffixes -> credibility degraded-to-unscored, skipped).
216	    # This pass runs ENTIRELY BEFORE compose (an ISOLATED flat phase — NO PG_PARALLEL_SECTIONS x
217	    # PG_COMPOSE_BASKET_WORKERS x inner-TPE nesting), so bounding its OWN loop concurrency is NOT the
218	    # multiplicative compose oversubscription the deadlock guard protects against. Parallelize the
219	    # member-verify loop and raise the side-judge cap FOR THIS PHASE ONLY (the designed I-deepfix-001
220	    # box2 lever; credibility_pass_concurrency RESTORES the compose-time cap of 8 before compose starts).
221	    # Faithfulness-neutral & UNDERCOUNT-only: the pass is ADVISORY (strict_verify / 4-role D8 /
222	    # span-grounding are untouched); verifying MORE members in LESS time yields STRICTLY MORE
223	    # corroboration than the 207-serial run and far more than the baseline's zero. All env-overridable.
224	    os.environ.setdefault("PG_CREDIBILITY_PASS_MAX_INFLIGHT", "16")
225	    os.environ.setdefault("PG_CREDIBILITY_PASS_SIDE_JUDGE_CONCURRENCY", "16")
226	    os.environ.setdefault("PG_CREDIBILITY_PASS_WALL_S", "600")
227	    # STEP 2: topic-driven, synthesis-enabling structure. Facet outline (thematic sections emerge
228	    # from the evidence) + the general research-report skeleton (intro / thematic bodies /
229	    # cross-study synthesis+contradictions / conclusions+gaps). GENERAL structural flags — they
230	    # hardcode no topic and are overridable from the environment.
231	    os.environ.setdefault("PG_FACET_OUTLINE", "1")
232	    os.environ.setdefault("PG_FACET_OUTLINE_SKELETON", "1")
233	    # STEP 3 (INSIGHT depth): make the cross-study synthesis section quantify agreement/disagreement
234	    # across the [ev]-backed body figures (enrich its evidence + directive). GENERAL structural
235	    # lever — role detected structurally, no topic/title hardcoded; strict_verify unchanged.
236	    os.environ.setdefault("PG_SYNTHESIS_QUANT_DIRECTIVE", "1")
237	    # STEP 4 (UTILIZATION — the basket under-utilization ghost): the live LLM outline lists only a
238	    # handful of ev_ids per section, so ~90% of the consolidated baskets reach NO section and never
239	    # compose a cited claim (measured 31/329 rendered; scripts/measure_utilization_route_all.py). Route
240	    # every ORPHAN basket to its best-matching thematic section by claim-vs-title content overlap (else a
241	    # single keep-all residual section). GENERAL, faithfulness-neutral: pure CONSOLIDATE placement —
242	    # drops no source, caps nothing; every routed basket's rendered sentence re-passes the UNCHANGED
243	    # strict_verify per clause. Deterministic A/B proved 31->328 baskets rendered. Also drop the
244	    # PG_MAX_EV_PER_SECTION row-cap ceiling so a facet keeps its full matched payload.
245	    os.environ.setdefault("PG_ROUTE_ALL_BASKETS", "1")
246	    os.environ.setdefault("PG_EV_BUDGET_TRACKS_PAYLOAD", "1")
247	
248	    corpus_path = Path(args.corpus)
249	    corpus = json.loads(corpus_path.read_text())
250	    corpus_rq = corpus["research_question"]
251	    if args.rq_drb_task:
252	        rq = _load_drb_prompt(args.rq_drb_task)
253	        log.info("RQ OVERRIDE: composing to DRB task %s verbatim prompt (corpus RQ kept as "
254	                 "provenance only). task_rq[:90]=%r", args.rq_drb_task, rq[:90])
255	    else:
256	        rq = corpus_rq
257	    evidence = corpus["evidence"]
258	    raw_clusters = corpus.get("finding_clusters") or []
259	    clusters = [SimpleNamespace(**c) if isinstance(c, dict) else c for c in raw_clusters]
260	    swg = corpus.get("same_work_groups")
261	    domain = corpus.get("domain", "")
262	
263	    run_id = time.strftime("agentic_report_%Y%m%d_%H%M%S")
264	    run_dir = ROOT / (args.out_dir or f"outputs/{run_id}")
265	    run_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
266	    log.info("corpus=%s  evidence=%d  clusters=%d  same_work_groups=%s  domain=%s",
267	             corpus_path.name, len(evidence), len(clusters),
268	             len(swg or []), domain or "(none)")
269	    log.info("PG_OUTLINE_AGENT=%s  out_dir=%s", os.getenv("PG_OUTLINE_AGENT"), run_dir)
270	
271	    from src.polaris_graph.generator.multi_section_generator import (  # noqa: PLC0415
272	        generate_multi_section_report,
273	    )
274	    from src.polaris_graph.llm.openrouter_client import (  # noqa: PLC0415
275	        PG_EVALUATOR_MODEL, PG_GENERATOR_MODEL,
276	    )
277	    from src.polaris_graph.outline.outline_agent import (  # noqa: PLC0415
278	        outliner_agent_model, outliner_code_model,
279	    )
280	
281	    dist = _tier_fractions(evidence)
282	    log.info("tier fractions: %s", {k: round(v, 3) for k, v in dist.items()})
283	    log.info("[gen] agent_model=%s code_model=%s generator=%s",
284	             outliner_agent_model(), outliner_code_model(), PG_GENERATOR_MODEL)
285	
286	    # STEP 4 (UTILIZATION): thread the PSL government-suffix list so the credibility pass RUNS
287	    # priors-only (judge=None under always-release => ZERO LLM scoring calls) and BUILDS the per-claim
288	    # baskets. Without gov_suffixes the pre-run guard DEGRADES to credibility_analysis=None (the
289	    # 794->9 collapse), which strands EVERY basket and makes PG_ROUTE_ALL_BASKETS inert — the report
290	    # then renders only the LLM-writer's directly-cited sources. Faithfulness-neutral: priors weights
291	    # are deterministic authority weights; strict_verify / 4-role D8 / span-grounding stay the ONLY
292	    # binding gates. Fail-open: an empty/unavailable suffix list leaves the legacy None path.
293	    _gov_suffixes = None
294	    try:
295	        from src.polaris_graph.authority.data_loader import load_authority_data  # noqa: PLC0415
296	        _gov_suffixes = tuple(load_authority_data().get("psl_gov_suffixes") or ()) or None
297	        log.info("[credibility] threaded psl_gov_suffixes=%d (priors-only basket build enabled)",
298	                 len(_gov_suffixes or ()))
299	    except Exception as _e:  # noqa: BLE001
300	        log.warning("[credibility] could not load psl_gov_suffixes (%s); credibility pass will "
301	                    "degrade to None and PG_ROUTE_ALL_BASKETS will be inert", _e)
302	
303	    # S4 render/compose projections (default-OFF): load a pinned gate artifact when
304	    # supplied and compile its deliverable/scope/compose views. Absent => every
305	    # projection is None => byte-identical champion composition + assembly.
306	    _gate_contract = None
307	    _deliverable_spec = None
308	    _scope_spec = None
309	    _compose_projection = None
310	    _render_plan: dict = {}
311	    _contract_sha = ""
312	    if args.gate_artifact:
313	        try:
314	            from src.polaris_graph.planning.planning_gate_schema import (  # noqa: PLC0415
315	                contract_from_dict,
316	            )
317	            from src.polaris_graph.planning.compose_render_projection import (  # noqa: PLC0415
318	                from_contract as _crp_from_contract,
319	            )
320	            _art = json.loads(Path(args.gate_artifact).read_text())
321	            _gate_contract = contract_from_dict(_art.get("contract") or {})
322	            _contract_sha = str(_art.get("contract_sha256") or "")
323	            _crp = _crp_from_contract(_gate_contract)
324	            _compose_projection = _crp  # exposes .voice_advisory()
325	            _render_plan = _crp.render_plan()
326	            # deliverable_spec surfaced to the outliner as required-section titles/order
327	            # (the S4 ORCH-2 seam already in _call_outline). scope_spec passthrough.
328	            _deliverable_spec = {
329	                "required_sections": _render_plan.get("required_titles", []),
330	                "document_type": _render_plan.get("document_type", ""),
331	            }
332	            _scope_spec = {}
333	            log.info("[gate] loaded artifact=%s contract_sha=%s required_sections=%d "
334	                     "doc_type=%r voice=%s",
335	                     args.gate_artifact, _contract_sha[:12],
336	                     len(_render_plan.get("required_titles", [])),
337	                     _render_plan.get("document_type", ""), _crp.has_voice())
338	        except Exception as _e:  # noqa: BLE001 — fail-open: no gate => champion path
339	            log.warning("[gate] could not load --gate-artifact %s (%s); running "
340	                        "byte-identical champion path (no projections)",
341	                        args.gate_artifact, _e)
342	            _gate_contract = None
343	            _deliverable_spec = _scope_spec = _compose_projection = None
344	            _render_plan = {}
345	
346	    t0 = time.time()
347	    multi = await generate_multi_section_report(
348	        research_question=rq,
349	        evidence=evidence,
350	        finding_clusters=clusters,
351	        same_work_groups=swg,
352	        section_temperature=0.3,
353	        outline_max_tokens=2500,
354	        section_max_tokens=2400,
355	        min_kept_fraction=0.4,
356	        max_parallel_sections=args.max_parallel,
357	        tier_fractions=dist,
358	        domain=domain,
359	        credibility_pass_gov_suffixes=_gov_suffixes,
360	        # S4: None (default, no --gate-artifact) => byte-identical to HEAD.
361	        deliverable_spec=_deliverable_spec,
362	        scope_spec=_scope_spec,
363	        compose_projection=_compose_projection,
364	    )
365	    dt = time.time() - t0
366	    kept = [s for s in multi.sections if not s.dropped_due_to_failure]
367	    log.info("[gen] elapsed=%.1fs  outline=%d sections  kept=%d  words=%s  "
368	             "verified=%s  dropped=%s  in_tok=%s out_tok=%s",
369	             dt, len(multi.outline), len(kept), getattr(multi, "total_words", "?"),
370	             getattr(multi, "total_sentences_verified", "?"),
371	             getattr(multi, "total_sentences_dropped", "?"),
372	             getattr(multi, "total_input_tokens", "?"),
373	             getattr(multi, "total_output_tokens", "?"))
374	    for sr in multi.sections:
375	        mark = "OK " if not sr.dropped_due_to_failure else "DROP"
376	        log.info("   [%s] %-42s verified=%s dropped=%s regen=%s",
377	                 mark, sr.title[:42], sr.sentences_verified,
378	                 sr.sentences_dropped, sr.regen_attempted)
379	
380	    # Persist the outline
381	    (run_dir / "multi_section_outline.json").write_text(
382	        json.dumps([{"title": p.title, "focus": p.focus, "ev_ids": p.ev_ids}
383	                    for p in multi.outline], indent=2, sort_keys=True) + "\n",
384	        encoding="utf-8")
385	
386	    # Assemble the JUDGED report body from VERIFIED text only.
387	    #  - Section headings are the generator's OWN topic-driven titles (facet outline + skeleton):
388	    #    an Introduction, thematic bodies, a Cross-Study Synthesis & Contradictions section, and a
389	    #    Conclusions & Research Gaps section — no clinical archetypes, no relabel map.
390	    #  - A single GENERAL, topic-neutral framing sentence under the title (NO factual claims / no
391	    #    numbers — pure presentation). The report's substantive framing lives in the generated
392	    #    Introduction section; this line only states the organizing method. The tripwire re-audits.
393	    title = args.title or _derive_title(rq)
394	    intro = (
395	        "This report synthesizes the retrieved research evidence on the question above. It is "
396	        "organized as a coherent review: an introduction that frames the scope, thematic sections "
397	        "that group the evidence by sub-topic, a cross-study synthesis that surfaces where the "
398	        "findings agree and conflict, and a closing discussion of conclusions and open research "
399	        "gaps. Every quantitative claim is span-grounded to a cited source; claims that could not "
400	        "be verified against the underlying evidence were removed rather than paraphrased."
401	    )
402	    # Verified section bodies (VERIFIED text only — never dropped/edited here).
403	    _verified = [
404	        sr for sr in multi.sections
405	        if not sr.dropped_due_to_failure and sr.verified_text
406	    ]
407	    # S4 contract-aware order: when the render plan names required section titles
408	    # in order, place matching verified sections FIRST in that order; every other
409	    # verified section keeps its original relative order AFTER them. This ORDERS
410	    # verified content — it never drops or fabricates a section. Absent render
411	    # plan => original order => byte-identical.
412	    _required_titles = list(_render_plan.get("required_titles", [])) if _render_plan else []
413	    if _required_titles:
414	        _verified = _order_sections_by_required(_verified, _required_titles)
415	    bodies: list[str] = [f"## {sr.title}\n\n{sr.verified_text}" for sr in _verified]
416	    sections_concat = "\n\n".join(bodies)
417	    if getattr(multi, "limitations_text", ""):
418	        sections_concat += f"\n\n## Limitations\n\n{multi.limitations_text}"
419	
420	    biblio = getattr(multi, "bibliography", []) or []
421	    # S4 references dedup by WORK: when the render plan requests it (default True
422	    # in a loaded contract), collapse bibliography rows that resolve to the same
423	    # underlying work (same url, else same statement). The FIRST row's number is
424	    # kept; this only removes DUPLICATE reference lines, never a distinct source,
425	    # and does not renumber in-prose markers. No gate artifact => biblio unchanged.
426	    _dedup_by_work = bool(_render_plan.get("references_dedup_by_work")) if _render_plan else False
427	    biblio_render = _dedup_biblio_by_work(biblio) if _dedup_by_work else biblio
428	    biblio_section = "\n\n## References\n"
429	    for b in biblio_render:
430	        biblio_section += (f"[{b.get('num')}] {str(b.get('statement',''))[:200]} — "
431	                           f"{b.get('url','')} (tier {b.get('tier','')})\n")
432	
433	    final_report = (f"# {title}\n\n{intro}\n\n{sections_concat}{biblio_section}")
434	    (run_dir / "report.md").write_text(final_report, encoding="utf-8")
435	
436	    # Pipeline telemetry / Methods is a SIDECAR artifact (provenance for us), NOT part of the judged
437	    # deliverable — a research report's reader does not want the generator's internal telemetry.
438	    tier_summary = ", ".join(f"{k}={v*100:.0f}%" for k, v in sorted(dist.items()))
439	    methods = (
440	        "# Methods / pipeline telemetry (sidecar — NOT part of the judged report.md)\n\n"
441	        f"Judged task: DRB task {args.rq_drb_task} (verbatim prompt).\n"
442	        f"Corpus RQ (provenance): {corpus_rq[:200]}...\n"
443	        f"Corpus: {corpus_path.name} ({len(evidence)} evidence rows, {len(clusters)} baskets; "
444	        f"domain={domain or 'general'}).\n"
445	        f"Outliner: AGENTIC (PG_OUTLINE_AGENT=1) — agent {outliner_agent_model()}, "
446	        f"code {outliner_code_model()}.\n"
447	        f"Generator: {PG_GENERATOR_MODEL} (multi-section: agentic outline + "
448	        f"{len(kept)} parallel verified sections + strict_verify + regen-on-failure).\n"
449	        f"Evaluator/mirror: {PG_EVALUATOR_MODEL}.\n"
450	        f"Tier distribution: {tier_summary}.\n"
451	    )
452	    (run_dir / "methods.md").write_text(methods, encoding="utf-8")
453	    (run_dir / "bibliography.json").write_text(
454	        json.dumps(biblio, indent=2, sort_keys=True) + "\n", encoding="utf-8")
455	
456	    # P0/proof: the agentic-outliner digest surfaced on MultiSectionResult — PROVE the deep render
457	    # stayed agentic (cp4_used='agentic'), NOT degraded-to-seed (mission metric-1).
458	    oa_stats = dict(getattr(multi, "outline_agent_stats", None) or {})
459	    cp4_used = str(oa_stats.get("cp4_used", "MISSING"))
460	    degraded_to_seed = bool(oa_stats.get("degraded_to_seed", False))
461	    degrade_reason = str(oa_stats.get("degrade_reason", ""))
462	    log.info("[agentic] cp4_used=%s degraded_to_seed=%s turns=%s degrade_reason=%r -> %s",
463	             cp4_used, degraded_to_seed, oa_stats.get("turns"), degrade_reason[:160],
464	             "AGENTIC" if cp4_used == "agentic" else "NOT-AGENTIC")
465	
466	    audit = _audit_citations(final_report, biblio)
467	    faithful = (audit["leaked_cite_ev_tokens"] == 0 and not audit["unresolved_markers"])
468	    log.info("[faithfulness] leaked_[CITE:ev]=%d  bib_markers_in_prose=%d  bib_entries=%d  "
469	             "unresolved_markers=%s -> %s",
470	             audit["leaked_cite_ev_tokens"], audit["distinct_bib_markers_in_prose"],
471	             audit["bibliography_entries"], audit["unresolved_markers"],
472	             "PASS" if faithful else "FAIL")
473	
474	    # S4 contract-compliance audit — DISCLOSURE-ONLY, ALONGSIDE (never in place of)
475	    # the citation audit above (which is untouched). It reads the finished report +
476	    # outline + biblio and reports term-level SATISFIED/FAILED/UNSATISFIABLE/UNKNOWN
477	    # per contract term with its owning stage. It NEVER drops/edits content and NEVER
478	    # touches strict_verify. retrieval_scope_status is recorded as
479	    # 'not_evaluated_prebuilt_corpus' because this driver runs on a PREBUILT corpus —
480	    # discovery never ran under the contract, so no scope requirement is claimed
481	    # satisfied. No --gate-artifact => no contract => an empty audit (fail-open).
482	    compliance = None
483	    if _gate_contract is not None:
484	        try:
485	            from src.polaris_graph.planning.contract_compliance import (  # noqa: PLC0415
486	                audit_contract,
487	            )
488	            outline_titles = [s.title for s in multi.sections
489	                              if not s.dropped_due_to_failure and s.verified_text]
490	            _ca = audit_contract(
491	                _gate_contract,
492	                final_report,
493	                outline=outline_titles,
494	                biblio=biblio,
495	                retrieval_scope_status="not_evaluated_prebuilt_corpus",
496	                contract_sha256=_contract_sha,
497	            )
498	            compliance = _ca.to_dict()
499	            (run_dir / "contract_compliance.json").write_text(
500	                json.dumps(compliance, indent=2) + "\n", encoding="utf-8")
501	            log.info("[compliance] counts=%s retrieval_scope_status=%s -> "
502	                     "contract_compliance.json",
503	                     compliance.get("counts"), compliance.get("retrieval_scope_status"))
504	        except Exception as _e:  # noqa: BLE001 — disclosure-only, never fails the run
505	            log.warning("[compliance] audit_contract failed (%s); skipping disclosure "
506	                        "(faithfulness + citation audit unaffected)", _e)
507	            compliance = None
508	
509	    summary = {
510	        "corpus": corpus_path.name,
511	        "judged_drb_task": args.rq_drb_task or None,
512	        "composed_to_rq": rq[:160],
513	        "corpus_rq": corpus_rq[:160],
514	        "report_title": title,
515	        "section_headings": [s.title for s in multi.sections
516	                             if not s.dropped_due_to_failure and s.verified_text],
517	        "evidence_rows": len(evidence),
518	        "baskets": len(clusters),
519	        "same_work_groups": len(swg or []),
520	        "outline_sections": len(multi.outline),
521	        "kept_sections": len(kept),
522	        "dropped_sections": len(multi.sections) - len(kept),
523	        "total_words": getattr(multi, "total_words", None),
524	        "total_sentences_verified": getattr(multi, "total_sentences_verified", None),
525	        "total_sentences_dropped": getattr(multi, "total_sentences_dropped", None),
526	        "bibliography_entries": len(biblio),
527	        "report_chars": len(final_report),
528	        "report_words": len(final_report.split()),
529	        "faithfulness_audit": audit,
530	        "faithfulness_pass": faithful,
531	        # S4 disclosure: retrieval scope was NOT evaluated (prebuilt corpus). The
532	        # contract-compliance audit (when a gate artifact was supplied) is a
533	        # SIDECAR disclosure — it never gates the run's return code.
534	        "retrieval_scope_status": "not_evaluated_prebuilt_corpus",
535	        "contract_compliance": compliance,
536	        "cp4_used": cp4_used,
537	        "degraded_to_seed": degraded_to_seed,
538	        "degrade_reason": degrade_reason[:200],
539	        "outline_agent_turns": oa_stats.get("turns"),
540	        "moat_quantified_models": len(getattr(multi, "quantified_models", None) or {}),
541	        "agent_model": outliner_agent_model(),
542	        "code_model": outliner_code_model(),
543	        "generator_model": PG_GENERATOR_MODEL,
544	        "elapsed_seconds": round(dt, 1),
545	        "out_dir": str(run_dir),
546	    }
547	    (run_dir / "compose_summary.json").write_text(
548	        json.dumps(summary, indent=2) + "\n", encoding="utf-8")
549	    log.info("WROTE %s (%d chars, %d words) + compose_summary.json",
550	             run_dir / "report.md", len(final_report), len(final_report.split()))
551	    print(json.dumps(summary, indent=2))
552	    return 0 if faithful else 1
553	
554	
555	if __name__ == "__main__":
556	    raise SystemExit(asyncio.run(main()))

1	"""
2	compute_ui_surface_sha.py — print sha256 of the UI surface (web/app/** + web/components/**).
3	
4	Used by both visual_review_gate.py (when emitting the audit) and the
5	codex-visual-required CI workflow (when verifying it). Single source
6	of truth for the algorithm; if it drifts, the audit breaks.
7	
8	The algorithm matches `git ls-files <paths> | sort | xargs sha256sum |
9	sha256sum` semantics but is pure-Python and works on the working tree
10	(not git-tracked content), so the writer sees the same SHA they'd see
11	on CI before committing.
12	
13	Excludes node_modules and .next (heavy / regenerated). Prints exactly
14	one 64-hex line on stdout; exits 0 always (a missing surface dir
15	yields an empty hash, which the gate treats as a separate error).
16	"""
17	
18	from __future__ import annotations
19	
20	import hashlib
21	import pathlib
22	import sys
23	
24	
25	UI_SURFACE_PATHS = ("web/app", "web/components")
26	
27	
28	def compute(repo_root: pathlib.Path) -> str:
29	    parts: list[bytes] = []
30	    for surface in UI_SURFACE_PATHS:
31	        root = repo_root / surface
32	        if not root.exists():
33	            continue
34	        for p in sorted(root.rglob("*")):
35	            if not p.is_file():
36	                continue
37	            if "node_modules" in p.parts:
38	                continue
39	            if ".next" in p.parts:
40	                continue
41	            rel = p.relative_to(repo_root).as_posix()
42	            h = hashlib.sha256()
43	            with p.open("rb") as f:
44	                for chunk in iter(lambda: f.read(65536), b""):
45	                    h.update(chunk)
46	            parts.append(f"{h.hexdigest()}  {rel}\n".encode("utf-8"))
47	    return hashlib.sha256(b"".join(parts)).hexdigest()
48	
49	
50	if __name__ == "__main__":
51	    repo_root = pathlib.Path(sys.argv[1]) if len(sys.argv) > 1 else pathlib.Path.cwd()
52	    print(compute(repo_root))
53

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""Deterministic OFFLINE converter: cp3 stage snapshot -> sweep-runtime cp4 corpus.
3	
4	The FULL-corpus cp4_used=agentic sweep (``scripts/outline_agentic_sweep.py``) loads a corpus in
5	the runtime schema:
6	
7	    {"research_question": str,
8	     "evidence": [ {evidence_id, tier, title, statement, source_url, ...}, ... ],
9	     "finding_clusters": [ {representative_index, member_indices, corroboration_count,
10	                            member_hosts, claim_group_id}, ... ],   # indices INTO ``evidence``
11	     "same_work_groups": [ {member_evidence_ids, canonical_index, same_work_id}, ... ],
12	     "domain": str, "basket_total": int}
13	
14	The canonical cp3 snapshot (``data/cp3_s3gear_329basket_snapshot.json``) instead stores baskets by
15	``member_evidence_ids`` (resolved evidence ids, NOT positional indices) and carries no evidence pool
16	of its own — the pool lives one stage upstream in the cp2 corpus snapshot's ``evidence_for_gen``
17	(996 rows) + ``retrieval.evidence_rows`` (the ONE referenced id, ``ev_621``, that the "for-gen"
18	pool demoted lives only here). This converter joins the two, deterministically, and:
19	
20	  * maps ``question`` -> ``research_question``;
21	  * rebuilds ``finding_clusters`` from ``payload.baskets`` — ``member_evidence_ids`` ->
22	    positional ``member_indices`` into the attached pool, ``representative_evidence_id`` ->
23	    ``representative_index``; carries ``corroboration_count`` / ``member_hosts`` / ``claim_group_id``;
24	  * attaches the full evidence pool (evidence_for_gen order, then any referenced id only present in
25	    retrieval appended at the tail — deterministic, worker-independent);
26	  * passes ``same_work_groups`` through untouched (they are keyed by ``member_evidence_ids`` and are
27	    consumed by ``_build_alias_map`` via Mapping access, so no index rewrite is needed);
28	  * FAILS CLOSED: every ``member_evidence_id`` referenced by any basket OR same-work group MUST
29	    resolve in the attached pool, else the converter raises (a silent partial corpus is forbidden).
30	
31	Durability: the supplemented evidence pool is snapshotted to
32	``data/cp2_evidence_pool_snapshot.json`` so a later run does not depend on the external
33	``/workspace/POLARIS/...`` path staying mounted; if the external cp2 snapshot is gone, the converter
34	rebuilds the corpus from that durable pool snapshot.
35	
36	Usage:
37	    python scripts/cp3_to_cp4_corpus.py                 # defaults below
38	    python scripts/cp3_to_cp4_corpus.py --cp3 <f> --cp2 <f> --out <f>
39	
40	Exit codes: 0 = wrote a fully-resolved corpus; 1 = a referenced id did not resolve (fail-closed).
41	"""
42	from __future__ import annotations
43	
44	import argparse
45	import json
46	import sys
47	from datetime import datetime, timezone
48	from pathlib import Path
49	from typing import Any
50	
51	ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
52	
53	_DEFAULT_CP3 = ROOT / "data" / "cp3_s3gear_329basket_snapshot.json"
54	_DEFAULT_CP2 = Path("/workspace/POLARIS/outputs/s2_hamster_i1/cp2_corpus_snapshot.json")
55	_DEFAULT_POOL_SNAPSHOT = ROOT / "data" / "cp2_evidence_pool_snapshot.json"
56	_DEFAULT_OUT = ROOT / "data" / "cp4_corpus_s3gear_329.json"
57	
58	
59	def referenced_evidence_ids(cp3: dict) -> set[str]:
60	    """Every evidence id the cp3 payload references — via baskets AND same-work groups.
61	
62	    ALL of these must resolve in the attached pool (fail-closed), because both the basket digest
63	    (member_indices) and the same-work alias fold (member_evidence_ids) read them downstream.
64	    """
65	    payload = cp3["payload"]
66	    ref: set[str] = set()
67	    for b in payload["baskets"]:
68	        ref.update(str(e) for e in b.get("member_evidence_ids") or [])
69	        rep = b.get("representative_evidence_id")
70	        if rep:
71	            ref.add(str(rep))
72	    for g in payload.get("same_work_groups") or []:
73	        ref.update(str(e) for e in g.get("member_evidence_ids") or [])
74	    return ref
75	
76	
77	def build_pool(cp2: dict, referenced: set[str]) -> tuple[list[dict], list[str]]:
78	    """Build the deterministic evidence pool from a cp2 corpus snapshot.
79	
80	    Pool = ``evidence_for_gen`` in its native order (the canonical for-generation pool), then any
81	    ``referenced`` id NOT present there appended from ``retrieval.evidence_rows`` (first occurrence,
82	    scanned in row order — deterministic). Returns (pool, still_missing).
83	    """
84	    efg = cp2.get("evidence_for_gen") or []
85	    pool: list[dict] = [dict(r) for r in efg if isinstance(r, dict) and r.get("evidence_id")]
86	    have = {str(r["evidence_id"]) for r in pool}
87	
88	    # supplement referenced ids the for-gen pool demoted, from the wider retrieval pool
89	    rows = (cp2.get("retrieval") or {}).get("evidence_rows") or []
90	    ret_by_id: dict[str, dict] = {}
91	    for r in rows:
92	        if isinstance(r, dict) and r.get("evidence_id"):
93	            ret_by_id.setdefault(str(r["evidence_id"]), dict(r))
94	    for eid in sorted(referenced - have):
95	        if eid in ret_by_id:
96	            pool.append(ret_by_id[eid])
97	            have.add(eid)
98	
99	    still_missing = sorted(referenced - have)
100	    return pool, still_missing
101	
102	
103	def build_cp4_corpus(cp3: dict, pool: list[dict]) -> dict:
104	    """Join cp3 baskets to the attached pool into the sweep-runtime corpus. FAILS CLOSED."""
105	    payload = cp3["payload"]
106	    baskets = payload["baskets"]
107	
108	    id2idx: dict[str, int] = {}
109	    for i, rec in enumerate(pool):
110	        id2idx.setdefault(str(rec["evidence_id"]), i)
111	
112	    referenced = referenced_evidence_ids(cp3)
113	    missing = sorted(r for r in referenced if r not in id2idx)
114	    if missing:
115	        raise ValueError(
116	            f"{len(missing)} referenced member_evidence_ids do not resolve in the attached pool "
117	            f"(fail-closed; refusing to write a partial corpus): {missing[:10]}"
118	            + (" ..." if len(missing) > 10 else "")
119	        )
120	
121	    clusters: list[dict] = []
122	    for b in baskets:
123	        member_ids = [str(e) for e in b.get("member_evidence_ids") or []]
124	        member_indices = [id2idx[e] for e in member_ids]
125	        rep_id = str(b.get("representative_evidence_id") or (member_ids[0] if member_ids else ""))
126	        clusters.append({
127	            "representative_index": id2idx[rep_id],
128	            "member_indices": member_indices,
129	            "corroboration_count": int(b.get("corroboration_count", len(member_indices))),
130	            "member_hosts": list(b.get("member_hosts") or []),
131	            "claim_group_id": b.get("claim_group_id"),
132	        })
133	
134	    corpus = {
135	        "research_question": cp3["question"],
136	        "domain": cp3.get("domain", ""),
137	        "evidence": pool,
138	        "finding_clusters": clusters,
139	        "same_work_groups": payload.get("same_work_groups") or [],
140	        "basket_total": len(clusters),
141	        "_provenance": {
142	            "converter": "scripts/cp3_to_cp4_corpus.py",
143	            "cp3_run_id": cp3.get("run_id"),
144	            "cp3_stage": cp3.get("stage"),
145	            "pool_size": len(pool),
146	            "referenced_ids": len(referenced),
147	            "created_utc": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
148	        },
149	    }
150	    return corpus
151	
152	
153	def _load_json(path: Path) -> dict:
154	    return json.loads(Path(path).read_text())
155	
156	
157	def convert(
158	    cp3_path: Path = _DEFAULT_CP3,
159	    cp2_path: Path = _DEFAULT_CP2,
160	    out_path: Path = _DEFAULT_OUT,
161	    pool_snapshot_path: Path = _DEFAULT_POOL_SNAPSHOT,
162	) -> dict:
163	    """End-to-end: load, build pool (with durability snapshot/fallback), join, write, return corpus."""
164	    cp3 = _load_json(cp3_path)
165	    referenced = referenced_evidence_ids(cp3)
166	
167	    cp2_path = Path(cp2_path)
168	    pool_snapshot_path = Path(pool_snapshot_path)
169	    if cp2_path.exists():
170	        cp2 = _load_json(cp2_path)
171	        pool, missing = build_pool(cp2, referenced)
172	        if missing:
173	            raise ValueError(
174	                f"cp2 snapshot {cp2_path} is missing {len(missing)} referenced ids "
175	                f"(cannot fail-closed on a partial pool): {missing[:10]}"
176	            )
177	        # snapshot the supplemented pool for durability (external cp2 path may vanish)
178	        pool_snapshot_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
179	        pool_snapshot_path.write_text(json.dumps({
180	            "source_cp2": str(cp2_path),
181	            "created_utc": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
182	            "referenced_ids": len(referenced),
183	            "evidence_pool": pool,
184	        }, indent=1))
185	    elif pool_snapshot_path.exists():
186	        snap = _load_json(pool_snapshot_path)
187	        pool = snap["evidence_pool"]
188	        print(f"[cp3_to_cp4] external cp2 {cp2_path} absent — using durable pool snapshot "
189	              f"{pool_snapshot_path} ({len(pool)} rows)")
190	    else:
191	        raise FileNotFoundError(
192	            f"neither the external cp2 snapshot ({cp2_path}) nor the durable pool snapshot "
193	            f"({pool_snapshot_path}) exists — cannot build the evidence pool"
194	        )
195	
196	    corpus = build_cp4_corpus(cp3, pool)
197	
198	    out_path = Path(out_path)
199	    out_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
200	    out_path.write_text(json.dumps(corpus, indent=1))
201	    return corpus
202	
203	
204	def main(argv: list[str] | None = None) -> int:
205	    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=__doc__,
206	                                 formatter_class=argparse.RawDescriptionHelpFormatter)
207	    ap.add_argument("--cp3", type=Path, default=_DEFAULT_CP3)
208	    ap.add_argument("--cp2", type=Path, default=_DEFAULT_CP2)
209	    ap.add_argument("--out", type=Path, default=_DEFAULT_OUT)
210	    ap.add_argument("--pool-snapshot", type=Path, default=_DEFAULT_POOL_SNAPSHOT)
211	    args = ap.parse_args(argv)
212	
213	    try:
214	        corpus = convert(args.cp3, args.cp2, args.out, args.pool_snapshot)
215	    except (ValueError, FileNotFoundError) as exc:
216	        print(f"FAIL-CLOSED: {exc}")
217	        return 1
218	
219	    prov = corpus["_provenance"]
220	    print(f"WROTE {args.out}")
221	    print(f"  research_question : {corpus['research_question'][:80]}...")
222	    print(f"  domain            : {corpus['domain']}")
223	    print(f"  pool_size         : {prov['pool_size']}")
224	    print(f"  referenced_ids    : {prov['referenced_ids']} (all resolved)")
225	    print(f"  basket_total      : {corpus['basket_total']}")
226	    print(f"  same_work_groups  : {len(corpus['same_work_groups'])}")
227	    return 0
228	
229	
230	if __name__ == "__main__":
231	    raise SystemExit(main())
232

1	"""
2	Playwright visual audit script for POLARIS dashboard.
3	Captures full-page and per-section screenshots for UI documentation.
4	"""
5	
6	import asyncio
7	from pathlib import Path
8	
9	from playwright.async_api import async_playwright
10	
11	
12	SECTION_IDS = [
13	    "overview",
14	    "planning",
15	    "search-fetch",
16	    "storm",
17	    "evidence",
18	    "verification",
19	    "iterations",
20	    "synthesis",
21	    "quality-gates",
22	    "llm-calls",
23	]
24	
25	
26	async def audit():
27	    output_dir = Path(r"C:\POLARIS\outputs\dashboard_audit")
28	    output_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
29	
30	    html_path = Path(r"C:\POLARIS\outputs\dashboard_PG_TEST_060_BTG.html")
31	    if not html_path.exists():
32	        print(f"ERROR: Dashboard file not found at {html_path}")
33	        return
34	
35	    url = f"file:///{html_path.as_posix()}"
36	    print(f"Opening: {url}")
37	
38	    async with async_playwright() as p:
39	        browser = await p.chromium.launch()
40	        page = await browser.new_page(viewport={"width": 1920, "height": 1080})
41	        await page.goto(url, wait_until="networkidle")
42	        await page.wait_for_timeout(1000)
43	
44	        # --- Screenshot 00: Full page, collapsed state ---
45	        await page.screenshot(
46	            path=str(output_dir / "00_full_page_collapsed.png"), full_page=True
47	        )
48	        print("Captured: 00_full_page_collapsed.png")
49	
50	        # --- Screenshot 01: Above the fold ---
51	        await page.screenshot(path=str(output_dir / "01_above_fold.png"))
52	        print("Captured: 01_above_fold.png")
53	
54	        # --- Expand ALL <details> elements ---
55	        details_count = await page.evaluate("""() => {
56	            const details = document.querySelectorAll('details');
57	            details.forEach(d => d.open = true);
58	            return details.length;
59	        }""")
60	        print(f"Expanded {details_count} <details> elements")
61	        await page.wait_for_timeout(500)
62	
63	        # --- Screenshot 02: Full page, all expanded ---
64	        await page.screenshot(
65	            path=str(output_dir / "02_full_page_expanded.png"), full_page=True
66	        )
67	        print("Captured: 02_full_page_expanded.png")
68	
69	        # --- Screenshot 03: Navigation sidebar ---
70	        nav = await page.query_selector("nav")
71	        if nav:
72	            box = await nav.bounding_box()
73	            if box:
74	                await page.screenshot(
75	                    path=str(output_dir / "03_nav_sidebar.png"),
76	                    clip={
77	                        "x": box["x"],
78	                        "y": box["y"],
79	                        "width": box["width"],
80	                        "height": min(box["height"], 1080),
81	                    },
82	                )
83	                print("Captured: 03_nav_sidebar.png")
84	
85	        # --- Screenshot 04: Header / meta bar ---
86	        header = await page.query_selector("header")
87	        if header:
88	            box = await header.bounding_box()
89	            if box:
90	                await page.screenshot(
91	                    path=str(output_dir / "04_header_meta.png"),
92	                    clip={
93	                        "x": box["x"],
94	                        "y": box["y"],
95	                        "width": min(box["width"], 1920),
96	                        "height": box["height"],
97	                    },
98	                )
99	                print("Captured: 04_header_meta.png")
100	
101	        # --- Per-section screenshots ---
102	        for idx, section_id in enumerate(SECTION_IDS):
103	            section = await page.query_selector(f"section#{section_id}")
104	            if not section:
105	                print(f"WARNING: Section #{section_id} not found, skipping")
106	                continue
107	
108	            await section.scroll_into_view_if_needed()
109	            await page.wait_for_timeout(300)
110	            box = await section.bounding_box()
111	            if box:
112	                # Clip height to max 6000px to avoid huge screenshots
113	                clip_height = min(box["height"], 6000)
114	                clip_width = min(box["width"], 1920)
115	                await page.screenshot(
116	                    path=str(
117	                        output_dir
118	                        / f"section_{idx + 1:02d}_{section_id.replace('-', '_')}.png"
119	                    ),
120	                    clip={
121	                        "x": max(box["x"], 0),
122	                        "y": max(box["y"], 0),
123	                        "width": clip_width,
124	                        "height": clip_height,
125	                    },
126	                )
127	                print(
128	                    f"Captured: section_{idx + 1:02d}_{section_id}.png "
129	                    f"({clip_width:.0f}x{clip_height:.0f})"
130	                )
131	            else:
132	                print(f"WARNING: No bounding box for section #{section_id}")
133	
134	        await browser.close()
135	
136	    # Print summary
137	    captured = sorted(output_dir.glob("*.png"))
138	    print(f"\n--- Audit Complete ---")
139	    print(f"Total screenshots: {len(captured)}")
140	    print(f"Output directory: {output_dir}")
141	    for f in captured:
142	        size_kb = f.stat().st_size / 1024
143	        print(f"  {f.name:45s} {size_kb:>8.1f} KB")
144	
145	
146	if __name__ == "__main__":
147	    asyncio.run(audit())
148

1	"""
2	DEEP GEMINI VERIFICATION — Tests that each Gemini-class feature actually works
3	with REAL LLM calls, REAL data, and REAL rendering.
4	
5	Unlike the smoke test (individual components) or fire test Layer 1 (local-only),
6	this script tests the INTEGRATION paths that produce Gemini-class output.
7	
8	Cost: ~$0.50-1.00
9	Time: ~5-10 minutes
10	
11	Usage:
12	    python scripts/deep_gemini_verify.py
13	"""
14	
15	import asyncio
16	import base64
17	import io
18	import json
19	import logging
20	import os
21	import re
22	import subprocess
23	import sys
24	import time
25	from pathlib import Path
26	
27	logging.basicConfig(level=logging.INFO, format="%(levelname)s %(message)s")
28	logger = logging.getLogger("deep_verify")
29	
30	PROJECT_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent
31	sys.path.insert(0, str(PROJECT_ROOT))
32	RESULTS = {"pass": 0, "fail": 0, "tests": []}
33	
34	
35	def record(name: str, passed: bool, detail: str = ""):
36	    status = "PASS" if passed else "FAIL"
37	    RESULTS["pass" if passed else "fail"] += 1
38	    RESULTS["tests"].append({"name": name, "status": status, "detail": detail})
39	    icon = "+" if passed else "!"
40	    logger.info(f"  [{icon}] {name}: {status} {detail}")
41	
42	
43	# ============================================================================
44	# BACKEND DEEP TESTS — Real LLM interaction
45	# ============================================================================
46	
47	
48	async def test_cluster_assessment():
49	    """Test 1: Does generate_structured() with ClusterAssessment actually work?
50	
51	    This is THE critical test — if ClusterAssessment fails, the pipeline
52	    can't decide which clusters become sections.
53	    """
54	    from src.polaris_graph.llm.openrouter_client import OpenRouterClient
55	    from src.polaris_graph.schemas import ClusterAssessment
56	
57	    client = OpenRouterClient()
58	
59	    evidence_text = (
60	        "Evidence 1: Epoxy coatings showed 45.2 MPa adhesion strength (ASTM D4541).\n"
61	        "Evidence 2: Urethane formulations achieved 38.7 MPa with 3% UV degradation.\n"
62	        "Evidence 3: Temperature cycling -40C to 85C reduced adhesion by 12-18%.\n"
63	        "Evidence 4: Cross-linked polymers outperform linear by 25-40%.\n"
64	        "Evidence 5: ISO 4624 and ASTM D4541 results comparable within 5%."
65	    )
66	
67	    prompt = (
68	        f"Assess whether this evidence cluster warrants a FULL_SECTION in a research report.\n\n"
69	        f"CLUSTER THEME: Adhesion strength comparison of polymer coatings\n"
70	        f"EVIDENCE COUNT: 5\n\n"
71	        f"EVIDENCE:\n{evidence_text}\n\n"
72	        f"Evaluate: quantity, diversity, data quality, and structured data presence."
73	    )
74	
75	    try:
76	        result = await client.generate_structured(
77	            prompt=prompt,
78	            schema=ClusterAssessment,
79	            system="You are a research analyst assessing evidence clusters.",
80	            max_tokens=1024,
81	        )
82	        valid_decisions = {"FULL_SECTION", "BRIEF", "MERGE", "DROP"}
83	        has_decision = result.decision in valid_decisions
84	        has_reasoning = len(result.reasoning) > 20
85	        has_claims = len(result.key_claims) >= 1
86	        has_data_flag = isinstance(result.has_structured_data, bool)
87	
88	        record(
89	            "cluster_assessment",
90	            has_decision and has_reasoning,
91	            f"decision={result.decision}, reasoning={len(result.reasoning)}ch, "
92	            f"claims={len(result.key_claims)}, has_data={result.has_structured_data}, "
93	            f"data_type={result.data_type}",
94	        )
95	    except Exception as exc:
96	        record("cluster_assessment", False, f"EXCEPTION: {str(exc)[:200]}")
97	
98	
99	async def test_structured_data_extraction():
100	    """Test 2: Does the LLM extract structured data from real content?
101	
102	    If this fails, chart generation NEVER triggers.
103	    """
104	    from src.polaris_graph.llm.openrouter_client import OpenRouterClient
105	    from src.polaris_graph.schemas import StructuredDataExtraction
106	
107	    client = OpenRouterClient()
108	
109	    content = (
110	        "The study compared removal efficiency of three filter types at different pH levels. "
111	        "At pH 7.0, activated carbon achieved 95% removal, ceramic filters reached 87%, and "
112	        "sand filters showed 72% removal. At pH 5.5, performance dropped: activated carbon 82%, "
113	        "ceramic 71%, sand 58%. The experiment was conducted at 25°C with a flow rate of 2.5 L/min. "
114	        "Over 12 months, activated carbon maintained 90% efficiency while ceramic degraded to 75%."
115	    )
116	
117	    prompt = (
118	        f"Extract ALL structured data points from this research content.\n"
119	        f"Each data point should have: label, value, unit, data_type, context.\n\n"
120	        f"CONTENT:\n{content}"
121	    )
122	
123	    try:
124	        result = await client.generate_structured(
125	            prompt=prompt,
126	            schema=StructuredDataExtraction,
127	            system="Extract numerical data points for visualization.",
128	            max_tokens=2048,
129	        )
130	        has_points = len(result.data_points) >= 3
131	        has_comparison = result.has_comparison_data
132	        has_values = all(dp.value for dp in result.data_points[:3]) if result.data_points else False
133	
134	        record(
135	            "structured_data_extraction",
136	            has_points and has_values,
137	            f"points={len(result.data_points)}, comparison={has_comparison}, "
138	            f"time_series={result.has_time_series}, "
139	            f"entities={result.comparison_entities[:3]}, "
140	            f"sample={result.data_points[0].label}={result.data_points[0].value} "
141	            f"({result.data_points[0].unit})" if result.data_points else "NO DATA",
142	        )
143	    except Exception as exc:
144	        record("structured_data_extraction", False, f"EXCEPTION: {str(exc)[:200]}")
145	
146	
147	async def test_evidence_first_section():
148	    """Test 3: Does the LLM produce citations, Key Findings, and tables
149	    when given the actual SECTION_SYSTEM_PROMPT with evidence?
150	
151	    This is the KEY test — if the LLM doesn't follow the prompt,
152	    no amount of code fixes will produce Gemini-class output.
153	    """
154	    from src.polaris_graph.llm.openrouter_client import OpenRouterClient
155	
156	    client = OpenRouterClient()
157	
158	    # Build evidence in the format the real pipeline uses
159	    evidence_pieces = [
160	        {"id": "ev_001", "tier": "GOLD", "statement": "Epoxy coatings showed 45.2 MPa adhesion (ASTM D4541)", "relevance": 0.95},
161	        {"id": "ev_002", "tier": "GOLD", "statement": "Urethane achieved 38.7 MPa with superior UV resistance (3% degradation)", "relevance": 0.92},
162	        {"id": "ev_003", "tier": "GOLD", "statement": "Temperature cycling -40C to 85C reduced adhesion 12-18% across materials", "relevance": 0.88},
163	        {"id": "ev_004", "tier": "SILVER", "statement": "Cross-linked formulations outperform linear by 25-40% in pull-off tests", "relevance": 0.82},
164	        {"id": "ev_005", "tier": "SILVER", "statement": "Silicone coatings: 29.1 MPa adhesion, best chemical resistance", "relevance": 0.78},
165	        {"id": "ev_006", "tier": "SILVER", "statement": "ASTM D4541 and ISO 4624 comparable within +/-5%", "relevance": 0.75},
166	        {"id": "ev_007", "tier": "BRONZE", "statement": "Nano-indentation provides sub-micron resolution for thin-film characterization", "relevance": 0.65},
167	        {"id": "ev_008", "tier": "BRONZE", "statement": "Humidity >85% RH for 500h causes 7-9% adhesion degradation", "relevance": 0.60},
168	    ]
169	
170	    evidence_text = "\n".join(
171	        f"[{e['id']}] ({e['tier']}, relevance={e['relevance']}) {e['statement']}"
172	        for e in evidence_pieces
173	    )
174	
175	    suggested_words = min(200 + len(evidence_pieces) * 80, 2000)
176	
177	    system_prompt = (
178	        "You are a research synthesis expert. Write evidence-based research sections.\n"
179	        "RULES:\n"
180	        "1. Every factual claim MUST cite evidence using [CITE:evidence_id] format\n"
181	        "2. Include a markdown comparison table if data supports it\n"
182	        "3. End with a **Key Findings:** subsection with 3-5 cited bullet points\n"
183	        "4. NO filler phrases (Furthermore, Moreover, Additionally, It is worth noting)\n"
184	        "5. Use precise data from evidence, not vague language"
185	    )
186	
187	    user_prompt = (
188	        f"Write a research section on: Material Comparison of Polymer Coatings\n\n"
189	        f"Target: approximately {suggested_words} words.\n\n"
190	        f"EVIDENCE (cite using [CITE:id]):\n{evidence_text}\n\n"
191	        f"Requirements:\n"
192	        f"- Include a comparison table with adhesion values\n"
193	        f"- End with **Key Findings:** bullet points\n"
194	        f"- Cite every claim with [CITE:ev_xxx]"
195	    )
196	
197	    try:
198	        response = await client.generate(
199	            prompt=user_prompt,
200	            system=system_prompt,
201	            max_tokens=4096,
202	            temperature=0.4,
203	        )
204	        content = response.content
205	
206	        word_count = len(content.split())
207	        cite_count = len(re.findall(r'\[CITE:', content))
208	        has_table = bool(re.search(r'\|[\s]*---', content))
209	        has_key_findings = bool(re.search(r'Key Findings', content, re.IGNORECASE))
210	        filler_words = ['Furthermore,', 'Moreover,', 'Additionally,', 'It is worth noting']
211	        filler_count = sum(content.count(w) for w in filler_words)
212	
213	        all_pass = cite_count >= 3 and has_key_findings
214	        record(
215	            "evidence_first_section",
216	            all_pass,
217	            f"words={word_count}, [CITE:]={cite_count}, table={has_table}, "
218	            f"key_findings={has_key_findings}, filler={filler_count}",
219	        )
220	
221	        # Sub-tests
222	        record("section_has_citations", cite_count >= 3,
223	               f"{cite_count} [CITE:] markers (need >=3)")
224	        # Tables come from analyze_structured_data() injection, not section writing.
225	        # This is informational — table absence from text-only evidence is expected.
226	        if has_table:
227	            record("section_has_table", True, "markdown table present (bonus)")
228	        else:
229	            logger.info("  [~] section_has_table: EXPECTED — tables come from structured data injection, not prose writing")
230	        record("section_has_key_findings", has_key_findings,
231	               "Key Findings section present" if has_key_findings else "NO KEY FINDINGS")
232	        record("section_low_filler", filler_count <= 2,

This file "deep_gemini_verify.py" — named after "Gemini-class" quality output, marketing-flavored "Gemini-class" terminology, and file name references "Gemini" but this is describing output quality tier, not the Gemini LLM provider. Notable: unprofessional docstring language "Gemini-class" (comparative marketing term), file name itself. Also candidate literal filenames "SHOWME_TEST_003.json" are external data references, not defined names in this file.

Let me flag the module docstring's "Gemini-class" phrase and the file name itself as a RENAME candidate (informal/marketing-flavored branding term used as taxonomy).

1	"""
2	Deep Visual Audit — Pixel-level screenshot capture for every view, tab, interaction, and screen size.
3	Captures screenshots for manual review. No auto-pass logic.
4	"""
5	
6	import asyncio
7	import sys
8	import os
9	from pathlib import Path
10	from playwright.async_api import async_playwright
11	
12	URL = "http://localhost:8771"
13	OUT = Path("logs/deep_audit_screenshots")
14	OUT.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
15	
16	VIEWPORTS = [
17	    ("desktop_1920", 1920, 1080),
18	    ("laptop_1440", 1440, 900),
19	    ("tablet_1024", 1024, 768),
20	    ("mobile_768", 768, 1024),
21	]
22	
23	
24	async def wait_loaded(page):
25	    """Wait for dashboard to fully load with events."""
26	    await page.wait_for_selector(".app-header", timeout=10000)
27	    await page.wait_for_timeout(2000)  # let SSE events arrive
28	
29	
30	async def click_nav(page, view_name):
31	    """Click a nav button by data-view attribute."""
32	    btn = page.locator(f'.nav-btn[data-view="{view_name}"]')
33	    await btn.click()
34	    await page.wait_for_timeout(800)
35	
36	
37	async def click_adv_tab(page, tab_text):
38	    """Click an advanced sub-tab by text content."""
39	    btn = page.locator(f'.adv-tab-btn:has-text("{tab_text}")')
40	    await btn.click()
41	    await page.wait_for_timeout(600)
42	
43	
44	async def screenshot(page, name, full_page=False):
45	    """Take a screenshot with a descriptive name."""
46	    path = OUT / f"{name}.png"
47	    await page.screenshot(path=str(path), full_page=full_page)
48	    print(f"  [{name}] saved ({path.stat().st_size // 1024}KB)")
49	
50	
51	async def audit_viewport(browser, vp_name, width, height):
52	    """Run full audit at a specific viewport size."""
53	    print(f"\n{'='*60}")
54	    print(f"VIEWPORT: {vp_name} ({width}x{height})")
55	    print(f"{'='*60}")
56	
57	    context = await browser.new_context(viewport={"width": width, "height": height})
58	    page = await context.new_page()
59	
60	    # Collect JS errors
61	    js_errors = []
62	    page.on("pageerror", lambda err: js_errors.append(str(err)))
63	
64	    await page.goto(URL)
65	    await wait_loaded(page)
66	
67	    # ── 1. Research View (default) ──
68	    print("\n[Research View]")
69	    await screenshot(page, f"{vp_name}_01_research_full")
70	
71	    # Scroll reasoning stream
72	    reasoning = page.locator(".reasoning-stream")
73	    if await reasoning.count() > 0:
74	        await reasoning.evaluate("el => el.scrollTop = el.scrollHeight")
75	        await page.wait_for_timeout(400)
76	        await screenshot(page, f"{vp_name}_02_research_scrolled")
77	        await reasoning.evaluate("el => el.scrollTop = 0")
78	
79	    # Expand a phase block if collapsed
80	    phase_headers = page.locator(".phase-block-header")
81	    count = await phase_headers.count()
82	    if count > 0:
83	        # Click first collapsed block
84	        first_block = page.locator(".phase-block:not(.expanded)").first
85	        if await first_block.count() > 0:
86	            await first_block.locator(".phase-block-header").click()
87	            await page.wait_for_timeout(400)
88	            await screenshot(page, f"{vp_name}_03_research_phase_expanded")
89	
90	    # Scroll pipeline column
91	    pipeline = page.locator(".pipeline-column")
92	    if await pipeline.count() > 0:
93	        await pipeline.evaluate("el => el.scrollTop = el.scrollHeight")
94	        await page.wait_for_timeout(400)
95	        await screenshot(page, f"{vp_name}_04_research_pipeline_scrolled")
96	        await pipeline.evaluate("el => el.scrollTop = 0")
97	
98	    # ── 2. Evidence View ──
99	    print("\n[Evidence View]")
100	    await click_nav(page, "evidence")
101	    await screenshot(page, f"{vp_name}_05_evidence_full")
102
