285	        value=32.0, unit="percent", context_snippet="32% weight loss", source_url="http://d")
286	    recs2 = detect_contradictions([d1, d2], is_clinical=True)
287	    hit = next((r for r in recs2 if not r.not_comparable and r.relative_difference > 0), None)
288	    _p(f"  same-unit 47% vs 32%: records={len(recs2)} "
289	       f"flagged={'yes' if hit else 'NO'} "
290	       f"{('rel=' + str(hit.relative_difference) + ' sev=' + hit.severity) if hit else ''}")
291	    _check(hit is not None,
292	           "a genuine same-unit 47% vs 32% contradiction MUST still be flagged (not over-suppressed)")
293	    _check(not hit.not_comparable, "the real contradiction must NOT be marked not_comparable")
294	
295	    # (iii) ITER-2 P1 REGRESSION (the new requirement): a UNIT-LESS SAME-METRIC contradiction with a
296	    # >1000% spread and NO count operand (hazard ratio 0.5 vs 8.0, rel 1500%, neither >= 100) must
297	    # STILL be flagged as a REAL contradiction. The iter-1 spurious-magnitude arm over-suppressed
298	    # exactly this; relabeling now REQUIRES positive count-scale evidence. Must hold.
299	    h1 = ExtractedNumericClaim(
300	        evidence_id="hr1", subject="drug_x", predicate="hazard ratio for all-cause mortality",
301	        value=0.5, unit="", context_snippet="hazard ratio 0.5", source_url="http://trial_a")
302	    h2 = ExtractedNumericClaim(
303	        evidence_id="hr2", subject="drug_x", predicate="hazard ratio for all-cause mortality",
304	        value=8.0, unit="", context_snippet="hazard ratio 8.0", source_url="http://cohort_b")
305	    recs3 = detect_contradictions([h1, h2], rel_threshold=0.5, abs_threshold=1.0, is_clinical=True)
306	    _check(len(recs3) == 1, f"unit-less HR 0.5 vs 8.0 must produce exactly one record, got {len(recs3)}")
307	    hr = recs3[0]
308	    _p(f"  iter-2 P1: unit-less HR 0.5 vs 8.0 -> not_comparable={hr.not_comparable} "
309	       f"rel={hr.relative_difference} pred={hr.predicate!r}")
310	    _check(hr.not_comparable is False,
311	           "iter-2 P1: unit-less same-metric >1000% with NO count operand must NOT be suppressed")
312	    _check("[not_comparable]" not in hr.predicate, "iter-2 P1: HR record must not carry the not_comparable tag")
313	    _check(hr.relative_difference > 10.0,
314	           "iter-2 P1: the real ~1500% (15.0) spread must be preserved, not nulled to 0.0")
315	
316	    # belt-and-suspenders: the LOCKED iter-2 P1 regression test must pass (2 passed)
317	    import subprocess
318	    proc = subprocess.run(
319	        [sys.executable, "-m", "pytest",
320	         "tests/polaris_graph/test_deepfix_contradiction_unitless_p1.py", "-q"],
321	        cwd=str(_REPO_ROOT), capture_output=True, text=True)
322	    out_tail = " | ".join((proc.stdout or "").strip().splitlines()[-2:])
323	    _p(f"  locked iter-2 P1 pytest: rc={proc.returncode}  {out_tail}")
324	    _check(proc.returncode == 0,
325	           f"locked iter-2 P1 regression test must pass (rc={proc.returncode})\n{proc.stdout}\n{proc.stderr}")
326	    _check("2 passed" in (proc.stdout or ""), "expected '2 passed' from test_deepfix_contradiction_unitless_p1")
327	
328	    _p("  -> PASS: scale-collapse + arXiv/law fragments killed (sources disclosed); 47%vs32% still flagged; "
329	       "iter-2 P1 unit-less HR 0.5/8.0 STILL flagged (2 passed)")
330	    return "PASS"
331	
332	
333	# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
334	# CANARY-1 — FIX-1 audit-map-from-partials (STRUCTURAL + settled-verdict backbone)
335	# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
336	def canary_1_audit_map_structural() -> str:
337	    _p("[CANARY-1] FIX-1 audit-map-from-partials (run_honest_sweep_r3.py seam-rescue) — STRUCTURAL")
338	    _check(_RUN_SWEEP.is_file(), f"run sweep file missing: {_RUN_SWEEP}")
339	    src = _RUN_SWEEP.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
340	
341	    marker = "I-deepfix-001 FIX-1 (#1344) AUDIT-MAP-FROM-PARTIALS"
342	    mpos = src.find(marker)
343	    _check(mpos >= 0, f"FIX-1 keystone marker not found: {marker!r}")
344	    # window: from the marker to the FourRoleEvaluationResult that closes the seam branch
345	    end = src.find("FourRoleEvaluationResult(", mpos)
346	    _check(end > mpos, "could not bound the FIX-1 block (FourRoleEvaluationResult sentinel not found after marker)")
347	    block = src[mpos:end]
348	    lineno = src[:mpos].count("\n") + 1
349	    _p(f"  located FIX-1 block at line {lineno}, window {len(block)} chars")
350	
351	    # (a) re-invokes the seam input builder
352	    fact_a = bool(re.search(r"four_role_input_builder\s*\(", block))
353	    # (b) writes the per-claim audit map to four_role_claim_audit.json
354	    fact_b = ('four_role_claim_audit.json' in block) and ('.write_text(' in block)
355	    # (c) folds unsettled kept claims as non-VERIFIED "UNADJUDICATED", settled verdicts WIN
356	    fact_c = ('"UNADJUDICATED"' in block) and bool(
357	        re.search(r"if\s+_seam_cid\s+not\s+in\s+_seam_partial_verdicts", block))
358	    # (d) fail-safe except preserving prior whole-body-withhold
359	    fact_d = bool(re.search(r"except\s+Exception", block)) and ("body-withhold" in block)
360	
361	    _p(f"  (a) re-invokes four_role_input_builder(...)            : {fact_a}")
362	    _p(f"  (b) writes four_role_claim_audit.json via write_text   : {fact_b}")
363	    _p(f"  (c) folds unsettled -> 'UNADJUDICATED' (settled wins)  : {fact_c}")
364	    _p(f"  (d) fail-safe except keeps prior whole-body-withhold   : {fact_d}")
365	    _check(fact_a, "FIX-1 must re-invoke four_role_input_builder on a torn seam")
366	    _check(fact_b, "FIX-1 must persist the re-derived map to four_role_claim_audit.json")
367	    _check(fact_c, "FIX-1 must fold unsettled kept claims as non-VERIFIED 'UNADJUDICATED' (never ship as verified)")
368	    _check(fact_d, "FIX-1 must have a fail-safe except preserving the prior whole-body-withhold")
369	
370	    # backbone tally: the 65-line settled-verdicts sidecar must be EXACTLY 58 VERIFIED + 7 UNSUPPORTED
371	    _check(_SETTLED_VERDICTS.is_file(), f"settled-verdicts artifact missing: {_SETTLED_VERDICTS}")
372	    tally = collections.Counter()
373	    nlines = 0
374	    for line in _SETTLED_VERDICTS.read_text(encoding="utf-8").splitlines():
375	        line = line.strip()
376	        if not line:
377	            continue
378	        nlines += 1
379	        tally[json.loads(line)["verdict"]] += 1
380	    _p(f"  settled verdicts: total={nlines} tally={dict(tally)}")
381	    _check(nlines == 65, f"settled-verdicts must be 65 lines, got {nlines}")
382	    _check(tally.get("VERIFIED") == 58, f"VERIFIED backbone must be 58, got {tally.get('VERIFIED')}")
383	    _check(tally.get("UNSUPPORTED") == 7, f"UNSUPPORTED must be 7, got {tally.get('UNSUPPORTED')}")
384	
385	    _p("  -> PASS: 4/4 code-structure facts hold + settled backbone is exactly 58 VERIFIED + 7 UNSUPPORTED")
386	    _p("  NOTE: FIX-1's BEHAVIORAL proof needs a FORCED-seam-tear VM resume (a clean resume won't")
387	    _p("        tear because FIX-2 prevents the verdict-budget TPM/context blowup that timed out the seam).")
388	    return "PASS"
389	
390	
391	def main() -> int:
392	    _p("=" * 78)
393	    _p("I-deepfix-001 (#1344) GATE B — OFFLINE behavioral preflight (no spend, no network)")
394	    _p(f"repo_root      = {_REPO_ROOT}")
395	    _p(f"artifact_dir   = {_ARTIFACT_DIR}  (exists={_ARTIFACT_DIR.is_dir()})")
396	    _p("=" * 78)
397	
398	    canaries = [
399	        ("CANARY-2 fix2_verdict_cap", canary_2_verdict_cap),
400	        ("CANARY-3 fix3_relevance_chrome", canary_3_relevance_chrome),
401	        ("CANARY-4 fix4_contradiction_gate", canary_4_contradiction_gate),
402	        ("CANARY-1 fix1_structural", canary_1_audit_map_structural),
403	    ]
404	    results: dict[str, str] = {}
405	    for name, fn in canaries:
406	        _p("")
407	        try:
408	            results[name] = fn()
409	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — report per-canary FAIL, keep going
410	            results[name] = "FAIL"
411	            _p(f"  -> FAIL: {type(exc).__name__}: {exc}")
412	
413	    _p("")
414	    _p("=" * 78)
415	    _p("SUMMARY")
416	    for name, verdict in results.items():
417	        _p(f"  {verdict:5} {name}")
418	    # overall: required = CANARY 2/3/4 PASS + CANARY-1 PASS; SKIP on CANARY-3 acceptable
419	    def ok(name: str) -> bool:
420	        v = results.get(name)
421	        return v == "PASS" or (name.startswith("CANARY-3") and v == "SKIP")
422	
423	    all_pass = all(ok(n) for n, _ in canaries)
424	    _p(f"  OVERALL = {'ALL_PASS' if all_pass else 'SOME_FAIL'}")
425	    _p("=" * 78)
426	    return 0 if all_pass else 1
427	
428	
429	if __name__ == "__main__":
430	    sys.exit(main())
431

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""Offline DeepTRACE self-scorer — I-deepfix-001 Wave-1c (#1344), TRIAGE PREDICTOR ONLY.
3	
4	================================  READ THIS FIRST  ================================
5	This script ESTIMATES a POLARIS rendered report's DeepTRACE citation-faithfulness score
6	OFFLINE, before spending on the paid GPT-5 / kimi-k2.6 judge. It is a **TRIAGE PREDICTOR,
7	NOT a pass/fail gate.** It never aborts, raises on bad input, blocks, or decides anything by
8	a hardcoded threshold (LAW VI). Its numbers are ESTIMATES, not a proven score. Per operator
9	directive ("prove our own scorer correct then trust it") the point of this module is
10	formula-fidelity: every metric below is a small pure function whose docstring quotes the exact
11	DeepTRACE formula so a reviewer can verify it. It does NOT replace the official paid harness —
12	it predicts it, cheaply, so a shallow/broken report can be caught before paid judging.
13	
14	DeepTRACE = Salesforce AI Research, arXiv 2509.04499. The 8 metrics are computed over a
15	report's statements × sources via a Citation matrix C and a Factual-support matrix F.
16	
17	HONEST LIMITATION — THE F-MATRIX IS SPAN-APPROXIMATE BY CONSTRUCTION
18	-------------------------------------------------------------------
19	DeepTRACE judges F over each source's FULL fetched text. We only have the banked
20	`corpus_snapshot.json` EXTRACTED SPANS (`direct_quote`) — median ~2.8K of ~14.7K chars,
21	~550/694 sources truncated (per REAL_PLAN_2026). So a statement that IS truly supported by a
22	source whose supporting sentence was not captured in the span reads as UNSUPPORTED here.
23	Net direction: this scorer UNDER-estimates support → OVER-estimates Unsupported-Statements,
24	UNDER-estimates Citation-Thoroughness. Sources whose bibliography URL/title resolves to no
25	snapshot span are `n_unreachable` (like DeepTRACE dropping ~15% un-scrapeable URLs) and can
26	never contribute F-support; they only DEFLATE Source-Necessity. Confidence ranking of the
27	predictions (stated so the reader weights them correctly):
28	  * BEST predicted (still span-approximate): Citation-Accuracy, Citation-Thoroughness,
29	    Unsupported-Statements, Uncited-Sources.
30	  * WEAK / PROXY: Relevant-Statement — core = an offline citation-presence PROXY for the paid
31	    GPT-5 core/filler `relevant[]` labels (unavailable offline); it WILL diverge from the paid
32	    Relevant-Statement and is the least-reliable estimate (flagged relevant_statements_ratio_is_proxy).
33	  * WEAKEST / usually N/A offline: One-Sided, Overconfident (need a stance + 1-5 confidence
34	    judge we do not run offline; None unless the caller supplies the labels).
35	
36	SOURCE-NECESSITY — FORMULA-FIDELITY CONFLICT (flagged for the Codex+Fable gate)
37	------------------------------------------------------------------------------
38	The Wave-1c task text names "Hopcroft-Karp → König min-VERTEX-cover". The primary sources use
39	min-SET-cover, and a triage predictor MUST compute the same quantity the paid path computes:
40	  * `scripts/dr_benchmark/deeptrace_scorer.py` (POLARIS paid path) = greedy min-SET-cover.
41	  * `third_party/answer-engine-eval/.../utils_coverage.greedy_set_cover` (official reference) =
42	    greedy set cover.
43	König two-sided min-vertex-cover (= max-matching size) OVER-states necessity on redundant
44	reports (statements {s1,s2,s3}; sources a={s1,s2}, b={s2,s3}, c={s1,s3}: set cover 2 → 2/3
45	correct, but max matching 3 → König 3 → 1.0 wrong). So the HEADLINE `source_necessity` uses
46	greedy min-set-cover (valid predictor), and Hopcroft-Karp→König min-vertex-cover is kept as the
47	LABELED diagnostic `source_necessity_mvc_diagnostic` (the algorithm the task names, implemented
48	+ tested). See `.codex/I-deepfix-001/wave1c_brief.md`.
49	
50	Fully disjoint: this script imports nothing from POLARIS except the NLI engine
51	`entails_directional` (imported LAZILY inside the default entailment wrapper, so importing this
52	module and `--help` never load torch / sentence-transformers). It modifies NO existing module.
53	"""
54	from __future__ import annotations
55	
56	import argparse
57	import json
58	import os
59	import re
60	import sys
61	from pathlib import Path
62	from typing import Any, Callable, Optional, Sequence
63	
64	# An entailment verdict function: (premise_span, hypothesis_statement) -> True / False / None.
65	# None = verdict unavailable (model/infra degrade) — counted as F=0 for scoring but tallied.
66	EntailFn = Callable[[str, str], Optional[bool]]
67	
68	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
69	# Parsing regexes (mirror scripts/dr_benchmark/pack_deeptrace.py conventions)
70	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
71	_CITE_RE = re.compile(r"\[([\d,\s]+)\]")           # [N] / [N, M] numbered citations
72	_EV_TOKEN_RE = re.compile(r"\[#ev:[^\]]*\]")       # POLARIS-internal provenance tokens (stripped)
73	_INTRA_LINE_SENT_RE = re.compile(r"(?<=[.!?\]])\s+(?=[A-Z0-9])")  # intra-line sentence boundary
74	_HEADING_RE = re.compile(r"^\s{0,3}#{1,6}\s")      # markdown ATX heading line
75	# Appendix boundary: the answer body is everything BEFORE the first of these (mirrors
76	# pack_drb2.answer_body). `---` is a horizontal rule that in POLARIS reports precedes the
77	# V30 disclosure appendix.
78	_APPENDIX_BOUNDARY_RE = re.compile(
79	    r"^\s{0,3}(##+\s*(bibliography|references|methods?|contradiction|"
80	    r"evidence[- ]support|v30\b)|-{3,}\s*$)",
81	    re.IGNORECASE,
82	)
83	# The exact redaction placeholder POLARIS emits where a claim failed 4-role verification. It is
84	# NOT an answer statement (it is a curator gap marker) and must be dropped from the statement set.
85	_REDACTION_MARKER = "did not survive"  # substring of the placeholder line; robust to wording drift
86	_BIB_LINE_RE = re.compile(r"^\s*\[(\d+)\]\s+(.*)$")  # a `## Bibliography` entry line
87	_BIB_TIER_RE = re.compile(r"\(tier\s+([^)]*)\)\s*$", re.IGNORECASE)
88	_URL_RE = re.compile(r"https?://\S+")
89	
90	
91	# ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
92	# THE 8 DEEPTRACE METRICS — pure functions, each docstring quotes the formula
93	# (BESTPRACTICE_2026_BRIEF DEEP PACK 1 / arXiv 2509.04499). C=citation matrix,
94	# F=factual-support matrix, ⊙=element-wise product, Σ=sum over all cells.
95	# ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
96	def one_sided_answer(has_pro: bool, has_con: bool) -> int:
97	    """Metric I — One-Sided (binary, debate queries only). LOWER better.
98	
99	    Formula: 0 if BOTH a pro AND a con statement are present, else 1.
100	    Offline we have no stance classifier; the caller supplies has_pro / has_con (else the
101	    orchestrator reports None = N/A)."""
102	    return 0 if (has_pro and has_con) else 1
103	
104	
105	def _confidence_is_overconfident(
106	    answer_confidence: Optional[int],
107	    statement_confidence: Optional[Sequence[Optional[int]]] = None,
108	) -> bool:
109	    """Whole-answer confidence==5 test — replicated EXACTLY from the paid path
110	    `scripts/dr_benchmark/deeptrace_scorer.py:_confidence_is_overconfident` (lines 122-131) so the
111	    triage Overconfident prediction matches the paid metric. If an explicit whole-answer
112	    confidence is provided it is AUTHORITATIVE (==5); OTHERWISE fall back to whether the MAX
113	    per-statement 1-5 confidence is 5 (the paper scores confidence statement-level). Exact
114	    short-circuit: a PROVIDED answer_confidence takes precedence and the statement branch is only
115	    consulted when answer_confidence is None (verified against the paid helper in the test)."""
116	    if answer_confidence is not None:
117	        return int(answer_confidence) == 5
118	    vals = [int(c) for c in (statement_confidence or []) if c is not None]
119	    return bool(vals) and max(vals) == 5
120	
121	
122	def overconfident_answer(
123	    one_sided: int,
124	    answer_confidence: Optional[int],
125	    statement_confidence: Optional[Sequence[Optional[int]]] = None,
126	) -> int:
127	    """Metric II — Overconfident (binary, debate queries only). LOWER better.
128	
129	    Formula: 1 if (One-Sided==1 AND whole-answer Confidence==5) else 0, where the confidence-5
130	    test is the paid path's `_confidence_is_overconfident` (answer_confidence==5, else max
131	    per-statement confidence==5). Confidence is a 1-5 Likert from a separate judge pass; None
132	    offline unless the caller supplies it."""
133	    return 1 if (one_sided == 1 and _confidence_is_overconfident(
134	        answer_confidence, statement_confidence)) else 0
135	
136	
137	def relevant_statement_ratio(n_core: int, n_total: int) -> float:
138	    """Metric III — Relevant Statement (ratio). HIGHER better.
139	
140	    Formula: (# relevant/core statements) / (total statements). 0.0 on empty."""
141	    return (n_core / n_total) if n_total else 0.0
142	
143	
144	def uncited_sources_ratio(n_cited: int, n_listed: int) -> float:
145	    """Metric IV — Uncited Sources (ratio, reported as %uncited). LOWER better.
146	
147	    Formula: (# listed − # cited) / (# listed) — the fraction of listed sources that NO
148	    statement cites. (The complement, cited/listed, is reported alongside.) 0.0 on empty."""
149	    return ((n_listed - n_cited) / n_listed) if n_listed else 0.0
150	
151	
152	def unsupported_statements_ratio(n_unsupported: int, n_relevant: int) -> float:
153	    """Metric V — Unsupported Statements (ratio). LOWER better.
154	
155	    Formula: (# unsupported statements) / (# relevant statements), where a relevant statement is
156	    unsupported iff NO listed source supports it in F (its F row is all-zero). 0.0 on empty."""
157	    return (n_unsupported / n_relevant) if n_relevant else 0.0
158	
159	
160	def source_necessity_ratio(cover_size: int, n_listed: int) -> float:
161	    """Metric VI — Source Necessity (ratio). HIGHER better.
162	
163	    Formula: (# necessary sources = |minimum source cover|) / (# listed sources). The HEADLINE
164	    cover is the greedy min-SET-cover over supported relevant statements (matches the paid
165	    `deeptrace_scorer.py` and the official `utils_coverage.greedy_set_cover`). A Hopcroft-Karp →
166	    König min-VERTEX-cover value is reported separately as a labeled diagnostic. 0.0 on empty."""
167	    return (cover_size / n_listed) if n_listed else 0.0
168	
169	
170	def citation_accuracy(sum_c_and_f: int, sum_c: int) -> float:
171	    """Metric VII — Citation Accuracy (= precision). HIGHER better.
172	
173	    Formula: Σ(C ⊙ F) / Σ(C). Of every citation the report makes, the fraction landing on a
174	    source that genuinely supports that statement. 0.0 when the report cites nothing."""
175	    return (sum_c_and_f / sum_c) if sum_c else 0.0
176	
177	
178	def citation_thoroughness(sum_c_and_f: int, sum_f: int) -> float:
179	    """Metric VIII — Citation Thoroughness (= recall). HIGHER better.
180	
181	    Formula: Σ(C ⊙ F) / Σ(F). Of every true (statement, source) support pair, the fraction the
182	    report actually cited. 0.0 when F has no support pair."""
183	    return (sum_c_and_f / sum_f) if sum_f else 0.0
184	
185	
186	# ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
187	# Source-cover algorithms
188	# ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
189	def greedy_min_set_cover(universe: set[int], source_sets: dict[int, set[int]]) -> list[int]:
190	    """Greedy minimum SET cover — the DeepTRACE Source-Necessity numerator (HEADLINE).
191	
192	    Mirrors `third_party/answer-engine-eval/.../utils_coverage.greedy_set_cover` and the paid
193	    `scripts/dr_benchmark/deeptrace_scorer.minimum_source_cover_size`: repeatedly take the source
194	    covering the most still-uncovered universe elements. Deterministic tie-break: highest new
195	    coverage, then LOWEST source index. Returns the chosen source indices (cover size = len).
196	    Guards against a non-coverable universe element (breaks on zero gain) so it never hangs."""
197	    covered: set[int] = set()
198	    cover: list[int] = []
199	    while covered != universe:
200	        best_idx: Optional[int] = None
201	        best_gain = 0
202	        for idx in sorted(source_sets):  # sorted => LOWEST-index tie-break is deterministic
203	            gain = len(source_sets[idx] - covered)
204	            if gain > best_gain:
205	                best_gain = gain
206	                best_idx = idx
207	        if best_idx is None or best_gain == 0:
208	            break  # no source covers a remaining element (unreachable when universe is covered)
209	        cover.append(best_idx)
210	        covered |= source_sets[best_idx]
211	    return cover
212	
213	
214	def _hopcroft_karp(adj: dict[int, list[int]], left: list[int], right: list[int]) -> dict[int, int]:
215	    """Hopcroft-Karp maximum bipartite matching. Returns match_right {right_vertex: left_vertex}.
216	
217	    `adj[u]` = list of right vertices adjacent to left vertex u. `left`/`right` = vertex id lists.
218	    Standard BFS-layered / DFS-augment implementation; O(E·sqrt(V))."""
219	    import collections
220	
221	    INF = float("inf")
222	    match_left: dict[int, Optional[int]] = {u: None for u in left}
223	    match_right: dict[int, Optional[int]] = {v: None for v in right}
224	    dist: dict[int, float] = {}
225	
226	    def bfs() -> bool:
227	        queue: "collections.deque[int]" = collections.deque()
228	        for u in left:
229	            if match_left[u] is None:
230	                dist[u] = 0
231	                queue.append(u)
232	            else:
233	                dist[u] = INF
234	        found = False
235	        while queue:
236	            u = queue.popleft()
237	            for v in adj.get(u, []):
238	                w = match_right[v]
239	                if w is None:
240	                    found = True
241	                elif dist[w] == INF:
242	                    dist[w] = dist[u] + 1
243	                    queue.append(w)
244	        return found
245	
246	    def dfs(u: int) -> bool:
247	        for v in adj.get(u, []):
248	            w = match_right[v]
249	            if w is None or (dist[w] == dist[u] + 1 and dfs(w)):
250	                match_left[u] = v
251	                match_right[v] = u
252	                return True
253	        dist[u] = INF
254	        return False
255	
256	    while bfs():
257	        for u in left:
258	            if match_left[u] is None:
259	                dfs(u)
260	    return {v: u for v, u in match_right.items() if u is not None}
261	
262	
263	def konig_min_vertex_cover(
264	    adj: dict[int, list[int]], left: list[int], right: list[int]
265	) -> tuple[set[int], set[int]]:
266	    """König's theorem: minimum VERTEX cover of a bipartite graph from a max matching.
267	
268	    DIAGNOSTIC ONLY (not the headline Source-Necessity — see the module docstring). Returns
269	    (cover_left, cover_right): a minimum vertex cover whose total size == max-matching size.
270	    Construction: let U = unmatched left vertices; alternating-walk (unmatched edges L→R, matched
271	    edges R→L) marks reachable Z; cover = (left − Z_left) ∪ (right ∩ Z_right)."""
272	    match_right = _hopcroft_karp(adj, left, right)          # {right: left}
273	    match_left = {u: v for v, u in match_right.items()}      # {left: right}
274	
275	    z_left: set[int] = set()
276	    z_right: set[int] = set()
277	    stack = [u for u in left if u not in match_left]         # unmatched left vertices
278	    z_left.update(stack)
279	    while stack:
280	        u = stack.pop()
281	        for v in adj.get(u, []):
282	            if v in z_right:
283	                continue
284	            # traverse an edge NOT in the matching (L→R)
285	            if match_left.get(u) == v:
286	                continue
287	            z_right.add(v)
288	            w = match_right.get(v)  # follow the matched edge R→L
289	            if w is not None and w not in z_left:
290	                z_left.add(w)
291	                stack.append(w)
292	    cover_left = {u for u in left if u not in z_left}
293	    cover_right = {v for v in right if v in z_right}
294	    return cover_left, cover_right
295

297	# ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
298	# Report / snapshot parsing (deterministic, offline)
299	# ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
300	def strip_provenance_tokens(text: str) -> str:
301	    """Remove POLARIS-internal `[#ev:...]` provenance tokens (DeepTRACE ignores markup)."""
302	    return _EV_TOKEN_RE.sub("", text)
303	
304	
305	def strip_citation_markup(text: str) -> str:
306	    """Remove `[N]` citation markers — the entailment hypothesis is the bare claim text
307	    (DeepTRACE's factual-support judge is told to ignore citations)."""
308	    return _CITE_RE.sub("", strip_provenance_tokens(text)).strip()
309	
310	
311	def extract_answer_body(report_md: str) -> str:
312	    """Everything BEFORE the first appendix boundary (## Bibliography / Methods / Contradiction /
313	    References / V30 / horizontal rule). Mirrors pack_drb2.answer_body; local so this is
314	    standalone. Provenance tokens stripped."""
315	    lines = report_md.splitlines()
316	    for i, line in enumerate(lines):
317	        if _APPENDIX_BOUNDARY_RE.match(line):
318	            lines = lines[:i]
319	            break
320	    return strip_provenance_tokens("\n".join(lines)).strip()
321	
322	
323	def split_statements(body: str) -> list[str]:
324	    """Decompose the answer body into the finest citation-bearing units (mirrors
325	    pack_deeptrace._split_statements): per markdown line → table cells (split on `|`) → prose
326	    sentences (terminal-punctuation boundary). Heading lines and the redaction placeholder are
327	    dropped. Real DeepTRACE uses a GPT-5 decomposition pass; this deterministic split is the
328	    disclosed triage approximation."""
329	    statements: list[str] = []
330	    for raw in body.splitlines():
331	        line = raw.strip()
332	        if not line or _HEADING_RE.match(line):
333	            continue
334	        # NOTE: the redaction placeholder is dropped at the SENTENCE level below, NOT the line
335	        # level — real POLARIS sections render as ONE multi-sentence line that carries an inline
336	        # redaction sentence, so a line-level drop would delete the whole section's real claims.
337	        units = [c for c in line.split("|") if c.strip()] if line.startswith("|") else [line]
338	        for unit in units:
339	            for seg in _INTRA_LINE_SENT_RE.split(unit):
340	                seg = seg.strip()
341	                if seg and _REDACTION_MARKER not in seg.lower():
342	                    statements.append(seg)
343	    return statements
344	
345	
346	def parse_bibliography(report_md: str) -> dict[int, dict[str, str]]:
347	    """Parse the `## Bibliography` section: {num: {'title', 'url', 'tier'}}. A bibliography entry
348	    line looks like `[N] Title — https://url (tier T1)`. URL/tier are optional (some entries have
349	    an empty URL). Only lines inside the Bibliography section are read."""
350	    refs: dict[int, dict[str, str]] = {}
351	    in_bib = False
352	    for line in report_md.splitlines():
353	        stripped = line.strip()
354	        if _HEADING_RE.match(line):
355	            in_bib = bool(re.match(r"^\s{0,3}##+\s*bibliography\b", line, re.IGNORECASE))
356	            continue
357	        if not in_bib:
358	            continue
359	        m = _BIB_LINE_RE.match(stripped)
360	        if not m:
361	            continue
362	        num = int(m.group(1))
363	        rest = m.group(2).strip()
364	        tier = ""
365	        tm = _BIB_TIER_RE.search(rest)
366	        if tm:
367	            tier = tm.group(1).strip()
368	            rest = rest[: tm.start()].strip()
369	        url_m = _URL_RE.search(rest)
370	        url = url_m.group(0).rstrip(").,") if url_m else ""
371	        title = rest
372	        if url:
373	            title = rest[: url_m.start()].strip()
374	        title = title.rstrip("—-–:").strip()
375	        refs[num] = {"title": title, "url": url, "tier": tier}
376	    return refs
377	
378	
379	def _normalize_url(url: str) -> str:
380	    u = (url or "").strip().lower()
381	    u = re.sub(r"^https?://", "", u)
382	    u = re.sub(r"^www\.", "", u)
383	    return u.rstrip("/")
384	
385	
386	def _normalize_title(title: str) -> str:
387	    t = (title or "").strip().lower().rstrip("….")
388	    return re.sub(r"\s+", " ", t)
389	
390	
391	def link_sources_to_spans(
392	    bibliography: dict[int, dict[str, str]], evidence: Sequence[dict]
393	) -> dict[int, Optional[str]]:
394	    """Resolve each bibliography number to its snapshot `direct_quote` span via normalized URL
395	    match first, then normalized title match. Unresolved → None (an `n_unreachable` source that
396	    can never contribute F-support). Purely deterministic."""
397	    by_url: dict[str, str] = {}
398	    by_title: dict[str, str] = {}
399	    for ev in evidence:
400	        span = (ev.get("direct_quote") or "").strip()
401	        if not span:
402	            continue
403	        u = _normalize_url(ev.get("source_url") or "")
404	        if u and u not in by_url:
405	            by_url[u] = span
406	        t = _normalize_title(ev.get("title") or ev.get("statement") or "")
407	        if t and t not in by_title:
408	            by_title[t] = span
409	    span_by_num: dict[int, Optional[str]] = {}
410	    for num, ref in bibliography.items():
411	        span = None
412	        u = _normalize_url(ref.get("url") or "")
413	        if u and u in by_url:
414	            span = by_url[u]
415	        if span is None:
416	            t = _normalize_title(ref.get("title") or "")
417	            if t and t in by_title:
418	                span = by_title[t]
419	        span_by_num[num] = span
420	    return span_by_num
421	
422	
423	def citation_columns(statements: Sequence[str]) -> list[set[int]]:
424	    """Per statement, the SET of source numbers it cites (parsed from `[N]` / `[N, M]`)."""
425	    out: list[set[int]] = []
426	    for s in statements:
427	        nums: set[int] = set()
428	        for grp in _CITE_RE.findall(s):
429	            for tok in grp.split(","):
430	                tok = tok.strip()
431	                if tok.isdigit():
432	                    nums.add(int(tok))
433	        out.append(nums)
434	    return out
435

437	# ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
438	# Default entailment wrapper — reuses the POLARIS NLI engine (lazy import)
439	# ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
440	def _default_entail_fn(premise_span: str, hypothesis_statement: str) -> Optional[bool]:
441	    """Production F-matrix verdict: does the source SPAN entail the STATEMENT? Reuses
442	    `src.polaris_graph.synthesis.consolidation_nli.entails_directional` (the ALCE / DeepTRACE
443	    citation direction — span→claim SUPPORT, forward logits only). Imported LAZILY so importing
444	    this script / running `--help` never loads torch. Returns True / False / None (None on any
445	    infra fault; the caller counts it as F=0 and tallies n_unknown_entailment)."""
446	    repo_root = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
447	    if str(repo_root) not in sys.path:
448	        sys.path.insert(0, str(repo_root))
449	    try:
450	        from src.polaris_graph.synthesis.consolidation_nli import entails_directional
451	    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — engine unavailable => UNKNOWN verdict, never crash
452	        print(f"[deeptrace_self_score] NLI engine unavailable ({exc}); F verdicts = UNKNOWN.",
453	              file=sys.stderr)
454	        return None
455	    try:
456	        return entails_directional(premise_span, hypothesis_statement)
457	    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — a verdict failure is UNKNOWN, never fatal (triage)
458	        print(f"[deeptrace_self_score] entailment failed ({exc}); verdict = UNKNOWN.",
459	              file=sys.stderr)
460	        return None
461	
462	
463	# ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
464	# Orchestrator — build C, F, compute all 8 metrics. Pure, never raises.
465	# ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
466	def compute_deeptrace_selfscore(
467	    *,
468	    statements: Sequence[str],
469	    bibliography: dict[int, dict[str, str]],
470	    span_by_num: dict[int, Optional[str]],
471	    entail_fn: EntailFn,
472	    is_debate: bool = False,
473	    has_pro: Optional[bool] = None,
474	    has_con: Optional[bool] = None,
475	    answer_confidence: Optional[int] = None,
476	    statement_confidence: Optional[Sequence[Optional[int]]] = None,
477	) -> dict[str, Any]:
478	    """Build the Citation matrix C and Factual-support matrix F from parsed inputs, then compute
479	    all 8 DeepTRACE metrics. PURE and offline; NEVER raises on degenerate/empty input (returns
480	    zero-filled metrics). `entail_fn(span, statement)` supplies each F verdict (stubbed in tests).
481	    """
482	    listed_nums = sorted(bibliography)
483	    n_listed = len(listed_nums)
484	    col_of = {num: k for k, num in enumerate(listed_nums)}
485	    n_stmt = len(statements)
486	
487	    cite_sets = citation_columns(statements)
488	    # Core/relevant heuristic: a statement is core iff it carries ≥1 citation to a LISTED source.
489	    relevant = [bool(cs & set(listed_nums)) for cs in cite_sets]
490	    n_relevant = sum(relevant)
491	
492	    # Citation matrix C (only citations to listed sources).
493	    C = [[0] * n_listed for _ in range(n_stmt)]
494	    for i, cs in enumerate(cite_sets):
495	        for num in cs:
496	            if num in col_of:
497	                C[i][col_of[num]] = 1
498	
499	    # Factual-support matrix F — F[i][j]=1 iff span j entails statement i (citation markup
500	    # stripped from the hypothesis). Computed over EVERY span-resolvable listed source, not just
501	    # cited ones (DeepTRACE judges support independent of citation).
502	    F = [[0] * n_listed for _ in range(n_stmt)]
503	    n_unknown_entailment = 0
504	    hypotheses = [strip_citation_markup(s) for s in statements]
505	    for j, num in enumerate(listed_nums):
506	        span = span_by_num.get(num)
507	        if not span:
508	            continue  # unreachable source: no span => contributes no F-support
509	        for i in range(n_stmt):
510	            hyp = hypotheses[i]
511	            if not hyp:
512	                continue
513	            verdict = entail_fn(span, hyp)
514	            if verdict is None:
515	                n_unknown_entailment += 1
516	            elif verdict:
517	                F[i][j] = 1
518	
519	    n_unreachable = sum(1 for num in listed_nums if not span_by_num.get(num))
520	
521	    # ── Metric III: Relevant Statement ────────────────────────────────────
522	    m_relevant = relevant_statement_ratio(n_relevant, n_stmt)
523	
524	    # ── Metric IV: Uncited Sources ────────────────────────────────────────
525	    cited_cols = {col_of[num] for cs in cite_sets for num in cs if num in col_of}
526	    n_cited = len(cited_cols)
527	    m_uncited = uncited_sources_ratio(n_cited, n_listed)
528	    cited_fraction = (n_cited / n_listed) if n_listed else 0.0
529	
530	    # ── Metric V: Unsupported Statements (over relevant statements) ────────
531	    n_unsupported = 0
532	    for i in range(n_stmt):
533	        if relevant[i] and not any(F[i]):
534	            n_unsupported += 1
535	    m_unsupported = unsupported_statements_ratio(n_unsupported, n_relevant)
536	
537	    # ── Metrics VII / VIII: Citation Accuracy / Thoroughness ──────────────
538	    sum_c = sum(sum(row) for row in C)
539	    sum_f = sum(sum(row) for row in F)
540	    sum_cf = sum(C[i][j] & F[i][j] for i in range(n_stmt) for j in range(n_listed))
541	    m_accuracy = citation_accuracy(sum_cf, sum_c)
542	    m_thoroughness = citation_thoroughness(sum_cf, sum_f)
543	
544	    # ── Metric VI: Source Necessity ───────────────────────────────────────
545	    # Universe = supported relevant statements; source_sets[j] = supported relevant statements j
546	    # supports. HEADLINE = greedy min-set-cover; DIAGNOSTIC = Hopcroft-Karp→König min-vertex-cover.
547	    universe: set[int] = set()
548	    source_sets: dict[int, set[int]] = {j: set() for j in range(n_listed)}
549	    for i in range(n_stmt):
550	        if not relevant[i]:
551	            continue
552	        supporters = [j for j in range(n_listed) if F[i][j]]
553	        if not supporters:
554	            continue
555	        universe.add(i)
556	        for j in supporters:
557	            source_sets[j].add(i)
558	    setcover = greedy_min_set_cover(universe, source_sets)
559	    setcover_size = len(setcover)
560	    m_necessity = source_necessity_ratio(setcover_size, n_listed)
561	    n_sole_supporter = sum(
562	        1 for j in range(n_listed)
563	        if any(len([k for k in range(n_listed) if F[i][k]]) == 1 and F[i][j]
564	               for i in range(n_stmt) if relevant[i])
565	    )
566	    # Diagnostic min-vertex-cover (König) over the same bipartite support graph.
567	    left = sorted(universe)
568	    right = [j for j in range(n_listed) if source_sets[j]]
569	    adj = {i: [j for j in range(n_listed) if F[i][j] and source_sets[j]] for i in left}
570	    cover_left, cover_right = konig_min_vertex_cover(adj, left, right)
571	    mvc_size = len(cover_left) + len(cover_right)
572	    m_necessity_mvc = source_necessity_ratio(mvc_size, n_listed)
573	
574	    # ── Metrics I / II: debate-only ───────────────────────────────────────
575	    one_sided: Optional[int] = None
576	    overconfident: Optional[int] = None
577	    if is_debate and has_pro is not None and has_con is not None:
578	        one_sided = one_sided_answer(bool(has_pro), bool(has_con))
579	        overconfident = overconfident_answer(one_sided, answer_confidence, statement_confidence)
580	
581	    return {
582	        "scorer": "polaris-offline-deeptrace-self-score",
583	        "role": "TRIAGE_PREDICTOR_ONLY",
584	        "is_estimate": True,
585	        "is_pass_fail_gate": False,
586	        "n_statements": n_stmt,
587	        "n_relevant_statements": n_relevant,
588	        "n_listed_sources": n_listed,
589	        "n_cited_sources": n_cited,
590	        "n_unreachable_sources": n_unreachable,
591	        "n_unknown_entailment_verdicts": n_unknown_entailment,
592	        # lower-better
593	        "one_sided": one_sided,
594	        "overconfident": overconfident,
595	        "uncited_sources_ratio": round(m_uncited, 4),
596	        "cited_sources_fraction": round(cited_fraction, 4),
597	        "unsupported_statements_ratio": round(m_unsupported, 4),
598	        # higher-better
599	        "relevant_statements_ratio": round(m_relevant, 4),
600	        # The paid path uses a SUPPLIED per-statement relevant[] bool from the GPT-5 core/filler
601	        # decomposition (deeptrace_scorer.py:149-152); that is NOT available offline, so this
602	        # ratio is an offline citation-presence PROXY that WILL diverge from the paid value.
603	        "relevant_statements_ratio_is_proxy": True,
604	        "source_necessity": round(m_necessity, 4),
605	        "source_necessity_cover_size": setcover_size,
606	        "source_necessity_mvc_diagnostic": round(m_necessity_mvc, 4),
607	        "source_necessity_mvc_size": mvc_size,
608	        "n_sole_supporter": n_sole_supporter,
609	        "citation_accuracy": round(m_accuracy, 4),
610	        "citation_thoroughness": round(m_thoroughness, 4),
611	        # matrices sums (for auditability)
612	        "sum_C": sum_c,
613	        "sum_F": sum_f,
614	        "sum_C_and_F": sum_cf,
615	        # honesty / provenance
616	        "source_necessity_interpretation": (
617	            "HEADLINE source_necessity = greedy min-set-cover / n_listed (matches the paid "
618	            "deeptrace_scorer.py + official utils_coverage.greedy_set_cover). "
619	            "source_necessity_mvc_diagnostic = Hopcroft-Karp->Konig min-vertex-cover / n_listed "
620	            "(the algorithm the Wave-1c task names; over-states necessity on redundant reports, "
621	            "so kept as a labeled diagnostic, NOT the headline)."
622	        ),
623	        "honest_limitation": (
624	            "F-matrix is SPAN-APPROXIMATE: judged over the snapshot direct_quote span, not the "
625	            "source's full text (~550/694 spans truncated). This UNDER-estimates support => "
626	            "OVER-estimates Unsupported, UNDER-estimates Citation-Thoroughness. "
627	            f"{n_unreachable} listed source(s) resolved to NO span (unreachable; deflate "
628	            "Source-Necessity). Relevant-Statement uses an offline citation-presence proxy for "
629	            "the paid GPT-5 core/filler relevant[] labels, which are unavailable offline; this "
630	            "ratio will diverge from the paid Relevant-Statement and is the least-reliable triage "
631	            "estimate (see relevant_statements_ratio_is_proxy=true). One-Sided/Overconfident need "
632	            "an offline stance+confidence judge we do not run (None unless supplied). "
633	            "Scores the full bibliography (no S1..S10 source cap), matching the named paid scorer "
634	            "deeptrace_scorer.py (which has no cap); the official EXTERNAL harness caps at 10 "
635	            "sources, so on reports citing >10 sources the Uncited-Sources and Source-Necessity "
636	            "ratios may differ from that external harness (Citation-Accuracy/Thoroughness are "
637	            "cap-insensitive). "
638	            "TRIAGE PREDICTION, NOT a proven score and NOT a pass/fail gate."
639	        ),
640	    }
641	
642	
643	def score_report(
644	    report_path: str | Path,
645	    snapshot_path: str | Path,
646	    *,
647	    entail_fn: Optional[EntailFn] = None,
648	    is_debate: bool = False,
649	    has_pro: Optional[bool] = None,
650	    has_con: Optional[bool] = None,
651	    answer_confidence: Optional[int] = None,
652	) -> dict[str, Any]:
653	    """Parse a rendered report.md + banked corpus_snapshot.json and return the triage metrics.
654	    `entail_fn=None` uses the production NLI engine (lazy). Never raises: on any read/parse error
655	    it returns a zero-filled result carrying an `error` note (triage must never block)."""
656	    ef = entail_fn or _default_entail_fn
657	    try:
658	        report_md = Path(report_path).read_text(encoding="utf-8", errors="replace")
659	    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
660	        report_md = ""
661	        _err = f"could not read report: {exc}"
662	    else:
663	        _err = ""
664	    try:
665	        snap = json.loads(Path(snapshot_path).read_text(encoding="utf-8", errors="replace"))
666	        evidence = snap.get("evidence_for_gen", []) if isinstance(snap, dict) else []
667	    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
668	        evidence = []
669	        _err = (_err + "; " if _err else "") + f"could not read snapshot: {exc}"
670	
671	    bibliography = parse_bibliography(report_md)
672	    statements = split_statements(extract_answer_body(report_md))
673	    span_by_num = link_sources_to_spans(bibliography, evidence)
674	    result = compute_deeptrace_selfscore(
675	        statements=statements,
676	        bibliography=bibliography,
677	        span_by_num=span_by_num,
678	        entail_fn=ef,
679	        is_debate=is_debate,
680	        has_pro=has_pro,
681	        has_con=has_con,
682	        answer_confidence=answer_confidence,
683	    )
684	    if _err:
685	        result["error"] = _err
686	    return result
687

691	    " POLARIS offline DeepTRACE self-scorer — TRIAGE PREDICTOR ONLY\n"
692	    " These numbers are ESTIMATES (span-approximate), NOT a proven score.\n"
693	    " This is NOT a pass/fail gate: it never blocks, aborts, or decides.\n"
694	    " Relevant-Statement is an offline citation-presence PROXY for the paid\n"
695	    " GPT-5 core/filler labels (unavailable offline) — the least-reliable line.\n"
696	    " Run it to predict the paid DeepTRACE judge cheaply, before spending.\n"
697	    "================================================================"
698	)

701	def _plain_summary(m: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
702	    def fmt(v: Any) -> str:
703	        return "n/a" if v is None else (f"{v:.3f}" if isinstance(v, float) else str(v))
704	    lines = [
705	        "Plain summary (higher/lower-better noted; all estimates):",
706	        f"  Relevant-Statement (higher better):   {fmt(m['relevant_statements_ratio'])}",
707	        f"  Uncited-Sources    (lower better):    {fmt(m['uncited_sources_ratio'])}",
708	        f"  Unsupported-Stmts  (lower better):    {fmt(m['unsupported_statements_ratio'])}",
709	        f"  Source-Necessity   (higher better):   {fmt(m['source_necessity'])}"
710	        f"   [mvc-diagnostic {fmt(m['source_necessity_mvc_diagnostic'])}]",
711	        f"  Citation-Accuracy  (higher better):   {fmt(m['citation_accuracy'])}",
712	        f"  Citation-Thorough. (higher better):   {fmt(m['citation_thoroughness'])}",
713	        f"  One-Sided/Overconf (debate, lower):   {fmt(m['one_sided'])} / {fmt(m['overconfident'])}",
714	        f"  statements={m['n_statements']} relevant={m['n_relevant_statements']} "
715	        f"listed_sources={m['n_listed_sources']} unreachable={m['n_unreachable_sources']} "
716	        f"unknown_entailment={m['n_unknown_entailment_verdicts']}",
717	    ]
718	    return "\n".join(lines)
719	
720	
721	def main(argv: Optional[Sequence[str]] = None) -> int:
722	    """CLI. Prints the triage header, the metrics JSON, and a plain summary. NEVER exits non-zero
723	    on a scoring problem (triage must not block a pipeline); returns 0 always for scoring paths."""
724	    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser(
725	        description=(
726	            "Offline DeepTRACE self-scorer — TRIAGE PREDICTOR ONLY (not a pass/fail gate). "
727	            "Estimates a POLARIS report's 8 DeepTRACE citation-faithfulness metrics from a "
728	            "rendered report.md + a banked corpus_snapshot.json, before paid GPT-5 judging. "
729	            "F-matrix is span-approximate by construction (see the module docstring)."
730	        )
731	    )
732	    ap.add_argument("--report", help="path to the rendered report.md")
733	    ap.add_argument("--snapshot", help="path to the banked corpus_snapshot.json")
734	    ap.add_argument("--out", default="", help="optional path to write the metrics JSON")
735	    ap.add_argument("--is-debate", action="store_true",
736	                    help="treat as a debate query (One-Sided/Overconfident need --has-pro/--has-con)")
737	    ap.add_argument("--has-pro", action="store_true", help="a supported pro statement is present")
738	    ap.add_argument("--has-con", action="store_true", help="a supported con statement is present")
739	    ap.add_argument("--confidence", type=int, default=None,
740	                    help="optional whole-answer 1-5 confidence for the Overconfident metric")
741	    args = ap.parse_args(argv)
742	
743	    print(_TRIAGE_HEADER)
744	    if not args.report or not args.snapshot:
745	        print("\nProvide --report and --snapshot to score. Example:\n"
746	              "  python scripts/deeptrace_self_score.py --report outputs/.../report.md "
747	              "--snapshot outputs/.../corpus_snapshot.json\n"
748	              "(--help for all options). Nothing scored; triage predictor is a no-op here.")
749	        return 0
750	
751	    result = score_report(
752	        args.report, args.snapshot,
753	        is_debate=args.is_debate,
754	        has_pro=args.has_pro if args.is_debate else None,
755	        has_con=args.has_con if args.is_debate else None,
756	        answer_confidence=args.confidence,
757	    )
758	    print("\n" + json.dumps(result, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False))
759	    print("\n" + _plain_summary(result))
760	    if args.out:
761	        try:
762	            Path(args.out).write_text(json.dumps(result, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False),
763	                                      encoding="utf-8")
764	            print(f"\n[deeptrace_self_score] wrote {args.out}")
765	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — a write failure must not fail the triage run
766	            print(f"\n[deeptrace_self_score] could not write --out ({exc}).", file=sys.stderr)
767	    return 0
768	
769	
770	if __name__ == "__main__":
771	    raise SystemExit(main())
772

1	"""End-to-end demo smoke script — validates the 5-slice FastAPI stack.
2	
3	Run before the Sep 6 tracer demo to confirm:
4	- create_app() boots without errors
5	- All 5 slice routes are mounted (intake, retrieval, generation,
6	  audit-bundle, benchmark)
7	- Each /health endpoint returns 200 with the expected slice id
8	- A canonical clinical question round-trips through process_intake and
9	  yields a ScopeDecision (not an IntakeError)
10	
11	Does NOT call OpenRouter or Serper — uses in-process TestClient and the
12	503-sentinel fall-throughs when env keys are missing. Cost: $0.
13	
14	Exit 0 = demo stack healthy. Exit 1 = at least one slice failing.
15	
16	Usage:
17	    PYTHONPATH=src python scripts/demo_smoke.py [-v]
18	"""
19	
20	from __future__ import annotations
21	
22	import argparse
23	import sys
24	from typing import Any
25	
26	from fastapi.testclient import TestClient
27	
28	from polaris_graph.api.intake import process_intake
29	from polaris_graph.scope.scope_decision import ScopeDecision
30	from polaris_v6.api.app import create_app
31	
32	
33	_EXPECTED_HEALTH = {
34	    "/api/intake/health": "slice_001_clinical_scope_discovery",
35	    "/api/retrieval/health": "slice_002_clinical_retrieval",
36	    "/api/generation/health": "slice_003_generator_strict_verify",
37	    "/api/audit-bundle/health": "slice_004_audit_bundle_export",
38	    "/api/benchmark/health": "slice_005_beat_both_benchmark",
39	}
40	
41	
42	_CANONICAL_QUESTION = (
43	    "Is high-dose aspirin effective for migraine in adults?"
44	)
45	
46	
47	def _check_health(client: TestClient, path: str, expected_slice: str,
48	                  verbose: bool) -> bool:
49	    try:
50	        r = client.get(path)
51	    except Exception as e:
52	        print(f"  FAIL {path}: exception {e}", file=sys.stderr)
53	        return False
54	    if r.status_code != 200:
55	        print(f"  FAIL {path}: status {r.status_code}", file=sys.stderr)
56	        return False
57	    body: dict[str, Any] = r.json()
58	    if body.get("slice") != expected_slice:
59	        print(
60	            f"  FAIL {path}: slice {body.get('slice')!r} != "
61	            f"{expected_slice!r}",
62	            file=sys.stderr,
63	        )
64	        return False
65	    if verbose:
66	        print(f"  OK   {path} -> slice={body['slice']}")
67	    return True
68	
69	
70	def _check_intake_pipeline(verbose: bool) -> bool:
71	    """Run a canonical question through process_intake.
72	
73	    Two modes per LAW II (no silent fallback):
74	    - With OPENROUTER_API_KEY in env: ASSERT scope_class == 'clinical_efficacy'.
75	      The canonical question MUST classify correctly under real keys; if it
76	      doesn't, the demo is broken and we fail loud.
77	    - Without keys: assert ScopeDecision shape only (LLM fallback returns
78	      'uncertain' which produces out_of_scope status — the documented
79	      keyless degradation).
80	    """
81	    import os
82	
83	    try:
84	        result = process_intake(_CANONICAL_QUESTION)
85	    except Exception as e:
86	        print(f"  FAIL process_intake: exception {e}", file=sys.stderr)
87	        return False
88	    if not isinstance(result, ScopeDecision):
89	        print(
90	            f"  FAIL process_intake: expected ScopeDecision, got {type(result).__name__}",
91	            file=sys.stderr,
92	        )
93	        return False
94	    if result.latency_ms > 30000:
95	        print(
96	            f"  FAIL process_intake: latency {result.latency_ms}ms > 30000ms",
97	            file=sys.stderr,
98	        )
99	        return False
100	    has_key = bool(os.environ.get("OPENROUTER_API_KEY", "").strip())
101	    if has_key:
102	        if result.scope_class != "clinical_efficacy":
103	            print(
104	                f"  FAIL process_intake: with OPENROUTER_API_KEY set, canonical "
105	                f"question expected scope_class='clinical_efficacy', got "
106	                f"{result.scope_class!r} (status={result.status})",
107	                file=sys.stderr,
108	            )
109	            return False
110	    if verbose:
111	        mode = "real-key" if has_key else "keyless"
112	        print(
113	            f"  OK   process_intake [{mode}] -> status={result.status} "
114	            f"scope_class={result.scope_class} "
115	            f"latency_ms={result.latency_ms}"
116	        )
117	    return True
118	
119	
120	def parse_args(argv: list[str]) -> argparse.Namespace:
121	    p = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="POLARIS 5-slice demo smoke")
122	    p.add_argument(
123	        "-v", "--verbose",
124	        action="store_true",
125	        help="Print per-check status",
126	    )
127	    return p.parse_args(argv)
128	
129	
130	def main(argv: list[str]) -> int:
131	    args = parse_args(argv)
132	    print("POLARIS demo smoke — 5-slice FastAPI stack")
133	    print("===========================================")
134	
135	    try:
136	        app = create_app()
137	    except Exception as e:
138	        print(f"FAIL: create_app() raised: {e}", file=sys.stderr)
139	        return 1
140	
141	    client = TestClient(app)
142	
143	    failures = 0
144	    print("[1/2] Health checks:")
145	    for path, expected in _EXPECTED_HEALTH.items():
146	        if not _check_health(client, path, expected, args.verbose):
147	            failures += 1
148	
149	    print("[2/2] Pipeline checks:")
150	    if not _check_intake_pipeline(args.verbose):
151	        failures += 1
152	
153	    print("===========================================")
154	    if failures:
155	        print(f"DEMO SMOKE FAILED: {failures} check(s) failed")
156	        return 1
157	    print("DEMO SMOKE PASSED: all 5 slices healthy + intake pipeline OK")
158	    return 0
159	
160	
161	if __name__ == "__main__":
162	    sys.exit(main(sys.argv[1:]))
163

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""I-run11-005: build a DEFENSIBLE Mirror labeled set for the robust re-bakeoff.
3	
4	The first bakeoff's grounded labels used `item.statement` (often a paper TITLE) → trivial claims
5	that produced non-deterministic noise. This builds real claim-sentences with MECHANICAL ground
6	truth (no hand-labeling bias):
7	  - GROUNDED pair  : a real factual sentence extracted from item_i's direct_quote, paired with
8	    item_i's direct_quote as the only doc. Ground truth = the sentence is a verbatim substring of
9	    the doc (verified). Indisputably grounded.
10	  - UNGROUNDED pair: the same sentence paired with a TOPICALLY-DISTANT item_j's direct_quote.
11	    Ground truth = the sentence is NOT a substring of doc_j (verified). Indisputably ungrounded.
12	
13	Boilerplate (cookie/marketing/URL/Title/section-header/too-short) is filtered OUT — that is NOT
14	truth-labeling, just excluding non-claims. SCOPE (honest): this set robustly measures BLANK-RATE +
15	FALSE-BIND + does-it-cite reliability (the swap-relevant safety metrics). It does NOT test nuanced
16	paraphrase/inference grounding judgment (verbatim claims are trivially findable) — that needs an
17	annotated set and is a follow-up. No live calls; deterministic. Run from repo root.
18	"""
19	from __future__ import annotations
20	import json
21	import re
22	from pathlib import Path
23	
24	POOL = Path("outputs/audits/I-run11-004/m25_bakeoff/evidence_pool.json")
25	OUT = Path("outputs/audits/I-run11-005/mirror_labeled_set.json")
26	
27	_BOILERPLATE = re.compile(
28	    r"cookie|marketing|url source|^title:|personalise|advertising|privacy|"
29	    r"^background$|^methods$|^results$|all rights reserved|©",
30	    re.IGNORECASE,
31	)
32	
33	
34	def factual_sentences(quote: str) -> list[str]:
35	    """Real factual sentences (>=45 chars, not boilerplate, not an ALL-CAPS header)."""
36	    text = quote.replace(chr(0x2013), "-").replace(chr(0x2014), "-")
37	    out = []
38	    for p in re.split(r"(?<=[.!?])\s+", text):
39	        p = p.strip()
40	        if len(p) < 45:
41	            continue
42	        if _BOILERPLATE.search(p):
43	            continue
44	        # skip ALL-CAPS headers / fragments with no lowercase
45	        if not any(c.islower() for c in p):
46	            continue
47	        out.append(p)
48	    return out
49	
50	
51	def main() -> None:
52	    pool = json.loads(POOL.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
53	    items = [
54	        x for x in pool
55	        if len(x.get("direct_quote") or "") > 200 and x.get("evidence_id")
56	    ]
57	    # normalize each doc the same way the claim is normalized so substring checks are consistent
58	    docs = {x["evidence_id"]: (x["direct_quote"].replace(chr(0x2013), "-").replace(chr(0x2014), "-"))
59	            for x in items}
60	    claims = []  # (evidence_id, sentence)
61	    for x in items:
62	        sents = factual_sentences(x["direct_quote"])
63	        if sents:
64	            claims.append((x["evidence_id"], sents[0]))  # one clean claim per item
65	
66	    grounded, ungrounded = [], []
67	    ids = [eid for eid, _ in claims]
68	    for idx, (eid, sent) in enumerate(claims):
69	        own_window = docs[eid][:2000]   # the EXACT text the model sees (bakeoff truncates to 2000)
70	        # GROUNDED: claim must be a substring of the TRUNCATED window the model actually receives,
71	        # else the model legitimately cannot find it and the "grounded" label would be corrupt.
72	        if sent in own_window:
73	            grounded.append({"claim": sent, "doc_id": eid, "doc_text": own_window,
74	                             "ground_truth": "grounded", "basis": "claim is a verbatim substring of the doc window"})
75	        # UNGROUNDED: sentence vs a rotated other doc's window (must NOT be a substring — verify).
76	        other = ids[(idx + 3) % len(ids)]
77	        other_window = docs[other][:2000]
78	        if other != eid and sent not in other_window:
79	            ungrounded.append({"claim": sent, "doc_id": other, "doc_text": other_window,
80	                               "ground_truth": "ungrounded", "basis": "claim absent from an unrelated doc window"})
81	
82	    dataset = {
83	        "_doc": "Defensible Mirror labeled set (I-run11-005). Mechanical ground truth (substring "
84	                "presence/absence). Scope: blank-rate + false-bind + cite-reliability; NOT nuanced "
85	                "paraphrase grounding (follow-up). Source: drb_72 m25_bakeoff evidence_pool.",
86	        "grounded": grounded,
87	        "ungrounded": ungrounded,
88	        "counts": {"grounded": len(grounded), "ungrounded": len(ungrounded)},
89	    }
90	    OUT.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
91	    OUT.write_text(json.dumps(dataset, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False), encoding="utf-8")
92	    print(f"wrote {OUT}")
93	    print(f"  grounded={len(grounded)}  ungrounded={len(ungrounded)}")
94	    print("  sample grounded :", grounded[0]["claim"][:80] if grounded else "NONE")
95	    print("  sample ungrounded:", ungrounded[0]["claim"][:80] if ungrounded else "NONE",
96	          "(vs doc", ungrounded[0]["doc_id"] + ")" if ungrounded else "")
97	
98	
99	if __name__ == "__main__":
100	    main()
101

1	"""I-arch-011 credibility-pass concurrency micro-test.
2	
3	Reproduces the box4 wedge in isolation: the credibility pass runs N member-verifies
4	through a ThreadPool(max_workers=max_inflight); each calls the entailment judge, which
5	spawns its OWN _post_with_total_deadline executor. box4 wedged at max_inflight=16 with
6	0 connections + 177 threads. This isolates the entailment-judge-under-concurrency
7	behavior so we pick the lowest deadlock-free max_inflight WITHOUT a 20-min full replay.
8	
9	Run on a VM (z-ai provider routing already deployed):
10	  cd /root/polaris_v2 && /root/run_env/bin/python -m scripts.diagnostics.credibility_concurrency_microtest
11	"""
12	import concurrent.futures
13	import os
14	import sys
15	import time
16	
17	sys.path.insert(0, "/root/polaris_v2")
18	
19	try:
20	    from dotenv import load_dotenv
21	
22	    load_dotenv("/root/polaris_v2/.env")
23	except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001
24	    pass
25	
26	# Bound a single hung call tighter than prod so a wedge surfaces fast in the test.
27	os.environ.setdefault("PG_ENTAILMENT_TOTAL_S", "40")
28	
29	from src.polaris_graph.llm.entailment_judge import _get_judge  # noqa: E402
30	
31	SENT = "Deep brain stimulation reduced motor symptoms in advanced Parkinson's disease."
32	SPAN = (
33	    "In a randomized controlled trial, deep brain stimulation of the subthalamic nucleus "
34	    "significantly improved motor function as measured by UPDRS-III compared with best "
35	    "medical therapy in patients with advanced Parkinson disease over 24 months of follow-up."
36	)
37	
38	PER_BATCH_TIMEOUT_S = 120
39	
40	
41	def _one(i: int):
42	    j = _get_judge()
43	    t0 = time.time()
44	    try:
45	        v = j.judge(SENT, SPAN)
46	        return (i, round(time.time() - t0, 1), "ok:" + str(v)[:24])
47	    except Exception as e:  # noqa: BLE001
48	        return (i, round(time.time() - t0, 1), "ERR:" + str(e)[:40])
49	
50	
51	def run_at(k: int) -> bool:
52	    """Return True if the batch COMPLETED (no wedge), False if it wedged."""
53	    m = max(k * 2, 4)  # ~2 waves at concurrency k
54	    t0 = time.time()
55	    done = []
56	    pool = concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=k)
57	    futs = [pool.submit(_one, i) for i in range(m)]
58	    try:
59	        for f in concurrent.futures.as_completed(futs, timeout=PER_BATCH_TIMEOUT_S):
60	            done.append(f.result())
61	    except concurrent.futures.TimeoutError:
62	        dt = time.time() - t0
63	        durs = sorted(d[1] for d in done)
64	        print(
65	            f"K={k:>2}: WEDGED — {len(done)}/{m} done in {dt:.0f}s "
66	            f"(completed call durations: {durs})",
67	            flush=True,
68	        )
69	        pool.shutdown(wait=False, cancel_futures=True)
70	        return False
71	    dt = time.time() - t0
72	    durs = sorted(d[1] for d in done)
73	    errs = sum(1 for d in done if d[2].startswith("ERR"))
74	    pool.shutdown(wait=True)
75	    print(
76	        f"K={k:>2}: OK {len(done)}/{m} in {dt:.1f}s | per-call durs={durs} | errs={errs}",
77	        flush=True,
78	    )
79	    return True
80	
81	
82	if __name__ == "__main__":
83	    print("=== credibility concurrency micro-test (entailment judge under ThreadPool) ===", flush=True)
84	    for k in (1, 4, 8, 16):
85	        ok = run_at(k)
86	        if not ok:
87	            print(f"FIRST_WEDGE_AT_K={k}", flush=True)
88	            # keep going to confirm higher levels also wedge? No — a wedge leaves stuck
89	            # threads; hard-exit so we don't hang the test process.
90	            os._exit(0)
91	    print("MICROTEST_DONE_no_wedge_through_16", flush=True)
92

1	"""I-arch-011 — LIVE behavioral gate: prove provider-rotation FIRES end-to-end on the REAL judge.
2	
3	Discriminator (no need to wait for a real z-ai blank window): lead the mirror chain with NOVITA, which
4	the call-shape bake-off proved HTTP-404s on the entailment judge's request shape (json_object). So:
5	  * rotation OFF -> novita 404 -> retries exhaust on novita -> ('ENTAILED','judge_error: ...') sentinel.
6	  * rotation ON  -> novita 404 -> ADVANCE to baidu -> a REAL ENTAILED verdict (reason != judge_error).
7	
8	FAILS LOUD (non-zero exit) if ON does not produce a real verdict — this is the §-1.4 behavioral harness:
9	the effect must APPEAR in the real output, not just be "in the slate".
10	
11	Run on a VM:
12	  cd /root/polaris_v2 && /root/run_env/bin/python -m scripts.diagnostics.entailment_rotation_behavioral
13	"""
14	import os
15	import sys
16	import tempfile
17	
18	sys.path.insert(0, "/root/polaris_v2")
19	try:
20	    from dotenv import load_dotenv
21	
22	    load_dotenv("/root/polaris_v2/.env")
23	except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001
24	    pass
25	
26	# Lead the mirror chain with novita (404s on json_object) so a CLEAN run is impossible WITHOUT rotation.
27	_TMP_YAML = os.path.join(tempfile.gettempdir(), "iarch011_rotate_routing.yaml")
28	with open(_TMP_YAML, "w", encoding="utf-8") as fh:
29	    fh.write(
30	        "roles:\n"
31	        "  mirror:\n"
32	        "    model: z-ai/glm-5.1\n"
33	        "    order: [novita, baidu, gmicloud]\n"
34	        "    ignore: [atlas-cloud, z-ai, siliconflow, parasail, chutes, phala]\n"
35	        "provider_aliases:\n"
36	        '  "Novita": novita\n'
37	        '  "Baidu": baidu\n'
38	        '  "GMICloud": gmicloud\n'
39	    )
40	os.environ["PG_PROVIDER_ROUTING_CONFIG"] = _TMP_YAML
41	os.environ["PG_OPENROUTER_PROVIDER_ROUTING"] = "1"
42	
43	from src.polaris_graph.benchmark import pathB_capture  # noqa: E402
44	from src.polaris_graph.llm import entailment_judge  # noqa: E402
45	from src.polaris_graph.roles import provider_routing  # noqa: E402
46	
47	provider_routing.reset_cache()
48	
49	SENT = "Deep brain stimulation improved motor function in advanced Parkinson's disease."
50	SPAN = (
51	    "In a randomized controlled trial, deep brain stimulation of the subthalamic nucleus significantly "
52	    "improved motor function as measured by UPDRS-III compared with best medical therapy in patients "
53	    "with advanced Parkinson disease over 24 months of follow-up."
54	)
55	
56	
57	def _run(rotate: bool):
58	    if rotate:
59	        os.environ["PG_JUDGE_PROVIDER_ROTATE"] = "1"
60	    else:
61	        os.environ.pop("PG_JUDGE_PROVIDER_ROTATE", None)
62	    # Resolve the mirror provider to the chain LEAD (novita), exactly as a live preflight would.
63	    token = pathB_capture.set_role_providers({"mirror": "novita"})
64	    try:
65	        judge = entailment_judge._EntailmentJudge()
66	        verdict, reason = judge.judge(SENT, SPAN)
67	    finally:
68	        pathB_capture.reset_role_providers(token)
69	    is_judge_error = isinstance(reason, str) and reason.startswith("judge_error:")
70	    print(f"  rotate={'ON ' if rotate else 'OFF'} -> verdict={verdict!r} "
71	          f"judge_error={is_judge_error} reason={reason[:60]!r}", flush=True)
72	    return verdict, is_judge_error
73	
74	
75	if __name__ == "__main__":
76	    if not os.environ.get("OPENROUTER_API_KEY", "").strip():
77	        print("FATAL: OPENROUTER_API_KEY not set", flush=True)
78	        sys.exit(2)
79	    print("=== entailment provider-rotation LIVE behavioral gate (novita-lead 404 -> baidu) ===", flush=True)
80	    off_verdict, off_jerr = _run(rotate=False)
81	    on_verdict, on_jerr = _run(rotate=True)
82	
83	    # The gate: ON must produce a REAL verdict (not judge_error). The OFF arm is informational — it SHOULD
84	    # judge_error on the novita-404 lead, but the binding assertion is that rotation RESCUES it.
85	    ok = (on_verdict == "ENTAILED") and (not on_jerr)
86	    if ok:
87	        print("ROTATION_BEHAVIORAL_PASS: rotation advanced off the novita-404 lead to a REAL ENTAILED verdict",
88	              flush=True)
89	        sys.exit(0)
90	    print("ROTATION_BEHAVIORAL_FAIL: rotation did NOT produce a real verdict on the novita-404 lead",
91	          flush=True)
92	    sys.exit(1)
93

1	"""I-arch-011 entailment-judge CALL-SHAPE bake-off (blank-rate AND verdict-correctness).
2	
3	The credibility-pass micro-test proved there is NO concurrency deadlock; the real chokepoint is a
4	GLM-5.1 BLANK-200 storm (empty-content 200s) when the entailment judge fires at
5	``reasoning:{effort:"high"}`` + ``response_format:{json_object}`` + max_tokens=131072. The yaml's own
6	2026-06-14 bake-off found the NUMERIC reasoning cap (``reasoning.max_tokens``), not effort=high, is
7	what makes GLM-5.1 return clean content.
8	
9	This bake-off A/Bs the exact prod call shape against the numeric-cap fix, on the REAL entailment
10	prompt, and gates on the RIGHT thing (advisor): a shape must be BOTH low-blank AND get all three
11	labeled pairs (ENTAILED / CONTRADICTED / NEUTRAL) correct — a shape that returns content but
12	rubber-stamps ENTAILED is strictly WORSE than the current blank->advisory-keep (it would relax the
13	NLI gate). Winner = lowest blank rate AND 3/3 labels correct on every sample.
14	
15	Run on a VM (z-ai-led mirror routing already deployed):
16	  cd /root/polaris_v2 && /root/run_env/bin/python -m scripts.diagnostics.entailment_shape_bakeoff
17	"""
18	import concurrent.futures
19	import json
20	import os
21	import sys
22	import time
23	
24	sys.path.insert(0, "/root/polaris_v2")
25	
26	try:
27	    from dotenv import load_dotenv
28	
29	    load_dotenv("/root/polaris_v2/.env")
30	except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001
31	    pass
32	
33	import httpx  # noqa: E402
34	
35	from src.polaris_graph.llm.entailment_judge import _ENTAILMENT_PROMPT  # noqa: E402
36	
37	API_KEY = os.environ.get("OPENROUTER_API_KEY", "").strip()
38	BASE = os.environ.get("OPENROUTER_BASE_URL", "https://openrouter.ai/api/v1").rstrip("/")
39	ENDPOINT = BASE + "/chat/completions"
40	MODEL = "z-ai/glm-5.1"
41	
42	# Three UNAMBIGUOUS labeled pairs (a correct NLI judge MUST get these right).
43	PAIRS = [
44	    # ENTAILED: conservative paraphrase, every assertion supported by the span.
45	    (
46	        "ENTAILED",
47	        "In a randomized controlled trial, deep brain stimulation of the subthalamic nucleus "
48	        "significantly improved motor function as measured by UPDRS-III compared with best medical "
49	        "therapy in patients with advanced Parkinson disease over 24 months of follow-up.",
50	        "Deep brain stimulation improved motor function in advanced Parkinson's disease.",
51	    ),
52	    # CONTRADICTED: span says improved, sentence says worsened.
53	    (
54	        "CONTRADICTED",
55	        "In the trial, deep brain stimulation significantly improved motor function compared with "
56	        "best medical therapy.",
57	        "Deep brain stimulation worsened motor function compared with best medical therapy.",
58	    ),
59	    # NEUTRAL: sentence introduces a new outcome (mortality) absent from the span.
60	    (
61	        "NEUTRAL",
62	        "Deep brain stimulation improved motor function in patients with advanced Parkinson disease.",
63	        "Deep brain stimulation reduced the risk of death in patients with advanced Parkinson "
64	        "disease.",
65	    ),
66	]
67	
68	# Call shapes. provider pinned to z-ai (allow_fallbacks:False) = prod single-provider behavior.
69	PROVIDER_BLOCK = {"order": [os.environ.get("PG_BAKEOFF_PROVIDER", "z-ai")],
70	                  "allow_fallbacks": False, "require_parameters": True}
71	SHAPES = {
72	    # S0 = current prod default (the blank-prone shape under test).
73	    "S0_effort_high_json": {"reasoning": {"effort": "high"},
74	                            "response_format": {"type": "json_object"}, "max_tokens": 131072},
75	    # S1 = LEAD FIX: numeric reasoning cap (matches the proven 2026-06-14 bake-off) + json_object ON.
76	    "S1_rcap100k_json": {"reasoning": {"max_tokens": 100000},
77	                         "response_format": {"type": "json_object"}, "max_tokens": 131072},
78	    # S2 = localize the json_object confound: numeric cap, json_object OFF.
79	    "S2_rcap100k_nojson": {"reasoning": {"max_tokens": 100000}, "max_tokens": 131072},
80	    # S3 = proven fallback: medium effort + json_object ON.
81	    "S3_effort_medium_json": {"reasoning": {"effort": "medium"},
82	                              "response_format": {"type": "json_object"}, "max_tokens": 131072},
83	    # S4 = tighter reasoning cap (more content headroom) + json_object ON.
84	    "S4_rcap8k_json": {"reasoning": {"max_tokens": 8000},
85	                       "response_format": {"type": "json_object"}, "max_tokens": 16000},
86	}
87	
88	SAMPLES_PER_PAIR = int(os.environ.get("PG_BAKEOFF_SAMPLES", "6"))
89	CALL_TIMEOUT_S = 150.0
90	
91	
92	def _extract_verdict(content: str):
93	    """Tolerant: pull the first {...} JSON object's `verdict`. Returns (verdict|None, why)."""
94	    s = (content or "").strip()
95	    if not s:
96	        return None, "BLANK"
97	    # strip code fences
98	    if s.startswith("```"):
99	        s = s.split("```", 2)[1] if s.count("```") >= 2 else s.strip("`")
100	        s = s.lstrip("json").strip()
101	    start = s.find("{")
102	    end = s.rfind("}")
103	    if start == -1 or end == -1 or end <= start:
104	        return None, "NO_JSON:" + s[:40]
105	    try:
106	        obj = json.loads(s[start:end + 1])
107	    except Exception as e:  # noqa: BLE001
108	        return None, "PARSE:" + str(e)[:40]
109	    v = str(obj.get("verdict", "")).strip().upper()
110	    if v not in ("ENTAILED", "NEUTRAL", "CONTRADICTED"):
111	        return None, "BADVERDICT:" + v[:24]
112	    return v, "ok"
113	
114	
115	def _one_call(shape_body: dict, span: str, sentence: str):
116	    prompt = _ENTAILMENT_PROMPT.format(span=span, sentence=sentence)
117	    body = {
118	        "model": MODEL,
119	        "messages": [{"role": "user", "content": prompt}],
120	        "temperature": 0.0,
121	        "provider": PROVIDER_BLOCK,
122	        **shape_body,
123	    }
124	    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {API_KEY}", "Content-Type": "application/json"}
125	    t0 = time.time()
126	    try:
127	        with httpx.Client(timeout=httpx.Timeout(CALL_TIMEOUT_S, connect=15.0, read=120.0)) as c:
128	            r = c.post(ENDPOINT, headers=headers, json=body)
129	        dt = round(time.time() - t0, 1)
130	        if r.status_code != 200:
131	            return ("HTTP%d" % r.status_code, None, dt)
132	        data = r.json()
133	        content = (data.get("choices") or [{}])[0].get("message", {}).get("content", "") or ""
134	        verdict, why = _extract_verdict(content)
135	        return (why if verdict is None else "ok", verdict, dt)
136	    except Exception as e:  # noqa: BLE001
137	        return ("EXC:" + str(e)[:40], None, round(time.time() - t0, 1))
138	
139	
140	def run_shape(name: str, shape_body: dict) -> dict:
141	    tasks = []
142	    for label, span, sentence in PAIRS:
143	        for _ in range(SAMPLES_PER_PAIR):
144	            tasks.append((label, span, sentence))
145	    results = [None] * len(tasks)
146	    with concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=6) as pool:
147	        futs = {pool.submit(_one_call, shape_body, sp, se): i
148	                for i, (lb, sp, se) in enumerate(tasks)}
149	        for fut in concurrent.futures.as_completed(futs):
150	            results[futs[fut]] = fut.result()
151	    # aggregate
152	    n = len(tasks)
153	    blanks = sum(1 for (why, v, _dt) in results if why == "BLANK")
154	    nonans = sum(1 for (why, v, _dt) in results if v is None)  # blank OR parse-fail OR http
155	    durs = sorted(dt for (_w, _v, dt) in results)
156	    median = durs[len(durs) // 2] if durs else 0.0
157	    per_label = {}
158	    for (label, _sp, _se), (why, verdict, _dt) in zip(tasks, results):
159	        d = per_label.setdefault(label, {"n": 0, "correct": 0, "nonans": 0})
160	        d["n"] += 1
161	        if verdict is None:
162	            d["nonans"] += 1
163	        elif verdict == label:
164	            d["correct"] += 1
165	    all_correct = all(d["correct"] == d["n"] for d in per_label.values())
166	    return {
167	        "name": name, "n": n, "blank_pct": round(100 * blanks / n, 0),
168	        "nonans_pct": round(100 * nonans / n, 0), "median_s": median,
169	        "per_label": per_label, "all_correct": all_correct,
170	        "sample_fails": [why for (why, v, _dt) in results if v is None][:6],
171	    }
172	
173	
174	if __name__ == "__main__":
175	    if not API_KEY:
176	        print("FATAL: OPENROUTER_API_KEY not set", flush=True)
177	        sys.exit(2)
178	    print(f"=== entailment call-shape bake-off | provider={PROVIDER_BLOCK['order'][0]} | "
179	          f"{SAMPLES_PER_PAIR} samples/pair x 3 labels ===", flush=True)
180	    rows = []
181	    for name, body in SHAPES.items():
182	        print(f"\n--- running {name} ---", flush=True)
183	        row = run_shape(name, body)
184	        rows.append(row)
185	        pl = row["per_label"]
186	        print(
187	            f"{name}: blank={row['blank_pct']:.0f}% nonans={row['nonans_pct']:.0f}% "
188	            f"median={row['median_s']}s | "
189	            f"E={pl.get('ENTAILED',{}).get('correct',0)}/{pl.get('ENTAILED',{}).get('n',0)} "
190	            f"C={pl.get('CONTRADICTED',{}).get('correct',0)}/{pl.get('CONTRADICTED',{}).get('n',0)} "
191	            f"N={pl.get('NEUTRAL',{}).get('correct',0)}/{pl.get('NEUTRAL',{}).get('n',0)} | "
192	            f"ALL_CORRECT={row['all_correct']} | fails={row['sample_fails']}",
193	            flush=True,
194	        )
195	    # winner = 3/3 labels perfect AND lowest non-answer rate
196	    clean = [r for r in rows if r["all_correct"] and r["nonans_pct"] == 0]
197	    clean.sort(key=lambda r: (r["nonans_pct"], r["median_s"]))
198	    print("\n=== SUMMARY ===", flush=True)
199	    for r in sorted(rows, key=lambda r: (r["nonans_pct"], not r["all_correct"], r["median_s"])):
200	        print(f"  {r['name']:<24} nonans={r['nonans_pct']:>3.0f}% blank={r['blank_pct']:>3.0f}% "
201	              f"median={r['median_s']:>5}s all_correct={r['all_correct']}", flush=True)
202	    if clean:
203	        print(f"WINNER={clean[0]['name']}", flush=True)
204	    else:
205	        passced = [r for r in rows if r["all_correct"]]
206	        if passced:
207	            passced.sort(key=lambda r: (r["nonans_pct"], r["median_s"]))
208	            print(f"NO_ZERO_BLANK_WINNER — best all-correct: {passced[0]['name']} "
209	                  f"(nonans={passced[0]['nonans_pct']:.0f}%)", flush=True)
210	        else:
211	            print("NO_WINNER — no shape got all 3 labels correct (investigate before any fix)", flush=True)
212

content.text: Note `passced` (line 205, 207) is a typo for "passed" — a real bug/typo but variable name misspelling, not exactly a "unprofessional" flag category per the task (marketing/temporal/informal), though a misspelled variable name is worth flagging as unprofessional/sloppy in external review. I'll include it as KEEP-NOTED... actually per the task, misspellings aren't explicitly listed but fall under general unprofessional bar. I'll flag as RENAME (typo).
